# MemoryTree Workflow **Persistent, Git-tracked project memory for AI coding assistants.** [![License: MIT](https://img.shields.io/badge/License-MIT-blue.svg)](LICENSE) [![Python 3.11+](https://img.shields.io/badge/Python-3.11%2B-3776AB.svg?logo=python&logoColor=white)](https://www.python.org/) [![Claude Code](https://img.shields.io/badge/Claude_Code-Skill-cc785c.svg?logo=anthropic&logoColor=white)](https://claude.ai/claude-code) [![Platform](https://img.shields.io/badge/Platform-macOS%20%7C%20Linux%20%7C%20Windows-lightgrey.svg)](#requirements)

Install  •  Usage  •  Features  •  Heartbeat  •  Docs

--- ## What It Does MemoryTree gives AI coding assistants (Claude Code, Codex, Gemini CLI) a structured memory layer that survives across sessions: | | Feature | Description | |---|---------|-------------| | :dart: | **Goals** | Track your project's north star across sessions | | :white_check_mark: | **Todos** | Version-bound task lists that stay in sync with goals | | :speech_balloon: | **Chat Logs** | Append-only session records for full traceability | | :books: | **Knowledge** | Durable notes, specs, and architecture decisions (also stored in goals when scope-relevant) | | :inbox_tray: | **Transcripts** | Import and archive AI chat history from Codex, Claude Code, and Gemini CLI | All stored as plain Markdown files under `Memory/`, tracked by Git, minimal dependencies. ## Install ```bash git clone https://github.com/beyondchenlin/memorytree-workflow ~/.claude/skills/memorytree-workflow ``` On Windows (Git Bash): ```bash git clone https://github.com/beyondchenlin/memorytree-workflow "$USERPROFILE/.claude/skills/memorytree-workflow" ``` ## Usage In any Claude Code session, run: ``` /memorytree-workflow ``` The skill will immediately: 1. **Detect** — check if the current repo has MemoryTree installed 2. **Alert** — display pending notifications from `~/.memorytree/alerts.json` 3. **Init** — if missing, scaffold `AGENTS.md` and `Memory/` folders 4. **Upgrade** — if partial, add missing pieces without overwriting existing policy 5. **Maintain** — if installed, read the active goal/todo and report current state No manual confirmation needed — the skill acts on the detection result immediately. ## Architecture ### Per-Repository Structure After initialization, your repo will have: ``` AGENTS.md # AI behavior rules for this repo Memory/ 01_goals/ # Versioned project goals 02_todos/ # Task lists bound to goals 03_chat_logs/ # Append-only session logs 04_knowledge/ # (optional) Durable project notes 05_archive/ # (optional) Retired goals/todos 06_transcripts/ # (optional) AI chat history archive clean/ # Cleaned Markdown indexes manifests/ # Import metadata raw/ # Raw transcript mirrors ``` ### Global Directory (`~/.memorytree/`) Cross-project shared state: ``` ~/.memorytree/ config.toml # User-level settings alerts.json # Pending notifications heartbeat.lock # Single-instance lock logs/ # Heartbeat execution logs transcripts/ # Global transcript archive raw/ # By client/project/year/month clean/ # Cleaned Markdown index/ sessions.jsonl # Session metadata search.sqlite # Full-text search index ``` For the full schema see [`references/global-configuration.md`](references/global-configuration.md). ## Features ### Memory Management | Memory Type | Behavior | |-------------|----------| | **Goals** | Changed only after explicit user confirmation of a scope or requirement change. Versioned. | | **Todos** | Kept in sync with the active goal. Updated when progress changes or next task becomes clearer. | | **Chat Logs** | Append-only. Never rewritten or deleted. | | **Knowledge** | Stores architecture decisions, product ideas, operating constraints. Goals may also hold scope-relevant items. | | **Archive** | Stale context moved here or to knowledge files to keep active files concise. | ### Multi-Client Transcript Import Import AI chat history from three clients: | Client | Source | |--------|--------| | **Codex** | `~/.codex/sessions/**/rollout-*.jsonl` | | **Claude Code** | `transcript_path` if exposed; otherwise `~/.claude/projects//*.jsonl` | | **Gemini CLI** | Saved chats first; checkpoints under `~/.gemini/tmp//checkpoints` if enabled | Key principles: - Raw transcripts preserved unchanged as source of truth - Cleaned Markdown generated by **deterministic code** (not model tokens) - Current repo only stores transcripts belonging to this project; others go to global archive only - First import asks once whether raw transcripts may be committed to the repo ### Cross-Project Search When searching all memories or chat history: 1. Query `search.sqlite` for fast full-text lookup 2. Use `sessions.jsonl` for session-level metadata filtering (client, project, date range) 3. Load matching cleaned Markdown for context; confirm exact wording against raw transcripts 4. Combine with the current repo's `Memory/` files 5. Fall back to scanning `clean/` directory if the index does not exist ### Session Continuity When you open a new session and want to continue where you left off, ask MemoryTree to show your most recent conversation: > "Look at my last chat" / "看看我最近的聊天" / "Continue where I left off" The skill will: 1. **Instant sync** — immediately scan all three client directories (not waiting for the scheduled heartbeat) 2. **Locate** — find the latest session for the current project across Claude Code, Codex, and Gemini CLI 3. **Summarize** — generate a continuation summary: what was discussed, what was tried, and what remains unresolved 4. **Handoff** — you can say "continue" and pick up exactly where the previous session stopped This works **cross-client**: a Codex session can be recovered from Claude Code, and vice versa. For details see [`references/transcript-archive.md`](references/transcript-archive.md) and [`references/heartbeat-scheduling.md`](references/heartbeat-scheduling.md). ### Git Safety MemoryTree follows strict isolation rules to never interfere with your repo's workflow: | Rule | Description | |------|-------------| | File isolation | Only stages/commits `Memory/**` (excluding `raw/` until approved) and `AGENTS.md` (when managed by MemoryTree) | | Branch isolation | Uses dedicated branch `memorytree/--` | | Commit format | `memorytree(): ` or repo-compatible equivalent | | PR isolation | Separate PR for MemoryTree-only changes | | Auto-merge | Only when repo still enforces its review and CI checks | | Auto-push | On by default; skips if no remote; fast-forward only | | Protected branches | Never overrides branch protection or CI rules | The skill stops and asks before proceeding in 8 scenarios: mixed product code, AGENTS.md conflicts, unclear target branch, repo forbids bot pushes, CI conflicts, unknown raw transcript permission, cross-project transcripts, or protected branch refusal. For the full policy see [`references/git-policy.md`](references/git-policy.md). ### Sensitive Info Scanning During transcript cleaning, the heartbeat scans for API keys, passwords, tokens, and other secrets: - Matches are logged as warnings and written to `alerts.json` - **No automatic deletion or redaction** — the user decides how to handle them ### Locale Support | Feature | Description | |---------|-------------| | Templates | English (`en`) and Simplified Chinese (`zh-cn`) | | Auto-detect | Detects language from repo content first, then system locale | | Reply language | Follows user explicit request > message language > repo locale > template locale > `en` | | Non-destructive | Never rewrites existing files just to translate them | ### Environment Adaptation MemoryTree does not assume the target repo has a Python environment: ``` Prefer uv run python → fall back to system python → fall back to direct template scaffolding ``` Never auto-creates `.venv` or `pyproject.toml` inside the project. ## Background Heartbeat The heartbeat automates transcript discovery, import, cleaning, commit, and push without consuming model tokens or requiring human intervention. **Execution flow**: ``` Acquire lock → Load config.toml → Iterate projects → Discover → Import → Clean → Commit → (auto_push) Push → Release lock → Exit ``` ### Install the daemon ```bash memorytree-daemon install ``` ### CLI commands | Command | Description | |-----------------------------|-------------| | `memorytree-daemon install` | Register the heartbeat with the OS scheduler (cron / launchd / Task Scheduler). | | `memorytree-daemon uninstall` | Remove the scheduled heartbeat task. | | `memorytree-daemon run-once` | Run a single heartbeat cycle immediately. | | `memorytree-daemon watch` | Continuous loop for development and debugging only. | | `memorytree-daemon status` | Show registration state and last execution result. | ### Key settings (`~/.memorytree/config.toml`) | Setting | Default | Description | |----------------------|----------|-------------| | `auto_push` | `true` | Push to remote after each commit. Skips if no remote is configured. | | `heartbeat_interval` | `"5m"` | Interval between heartbeat executions. | | `log_level` | `"info"` | Log verbosity (`debug`, `info`, `warn`, `error`). | ### Design constraints - Single execution, stateless, idempotent — safe to re-run at any interval - One computer = one scheduled heartbeat task - Will not silently register — always confirms before `install` For details see [`references/heartbeat-scheduling.md`](references/heartbeat-scheduling.md) and [`references/global-configuration.md`](references/global-configuration.md). ## Design Principles | Principle | How | |-----------|-----| | **Zero intrusion** | Never changes the repo's branch model, CI, or review process | | **Minimal dependencies** | Scripts target Python standard library; no third-party packages required at install time | | **Deterministic** | Transcript cleaning done by code, not model tokens | | **Single source of truth** | Each concept defined in one reference file, others cross-reference | | **Spec-driven** | Reference docs define the contract; code implements it faithfully | | **User asset** | Memory belongs to the user; auto_push on by default to prevent local data loss | ## Reference Docs | File | Purpose | |------|---------| | [`references/project-detection.md`](references/project-detection.md) | Detect install state | | [`references/memory-layout.md`](references/memory-layout.md) | Folder and file naming rules | | [`references/update-rules.md`](references/update-rules.md) | When to version goals, todos, and session logs | | [`references/git-policy.md`](references/git-policy.md) | Git defaults, auto-push, and policy merge | | [`references/heartbeat-scheduling.md`](references/heartbeat-scheduling.md) | Heartbeat architecture, daemon CLI, execution flow | | [`references/global-configuration.md`](references/global-configuration.md) | `~/.memorytree/` layout, `config.toml` schema, `alerts.json` format | | [`references/transcript-archive.md`](references/transcript-archive.md) | Transcript archival, cleaning, search, and upload rules | | [`references/locale-selection.md`](references/locale-selection.md) | Locale choice rules | | [`references/response-language.md`](references/response-language.md) | Reply-language precedence | | [`references/execution-environment.md`](references/execution-environment.md) | How to run scripts without a project-local Python environment | | [`references/upgrade-path.md`](references/upgrade-path.md) | Safe adoption flow for partial repos | ## Update ```bash cd ~/.claude/skills/memorytree-workflow && git pull ``` ## Requirements - [Claude Code](https://claude.ai/claude-code) CLI - Python 3.11+ (for transcript and heartbeat scripts; `tomllib` is stdlib in 3.11+) - Git ## License MIT

AI behavior rules for this repo 76→Memory/ 77→ 01_goals/ # Versioned project goals 78→ 02_todos/ # Task lists bound to goals 79→ 03_chat_logs/ # Append-only session logs 80→ 04_knowledge/ # (optional) Durable project notes 81→ 05_archive/ # (optional) Retired goals/todos 82→ 06_transcripts/ # (optional) AI chat history archive 83→ clean/ # Cleaned Markdown indexes 84→ manifests/ # Import metadata 85→ raw/ # Raw transcript mirrors 86→``` 87→ 88→### Global Directory (`~/.memorytree/`) 89→ 90→Cross-project shared state: 91→ 92→``` 93→~/.memorytree/ 94→ config.toml # User-level settings 95→ alerts.json # Pending notifications 96→ heartbeat.lock # Single-instance lock 97→ logs/ # Heartbeat execution logs 98→ transcripts/ # Global transcript archive 99→ raw/ # By client/project/year/month 100→ clean/ # Cleaned Markdown 101→ index/ 102→ sessions.jsonl # Session metadata 103→ search.sqlite # Full-text search index 104→``` 105→ 106→For the full schema see [`references/global-configuration.md`](references/global-configuration.md). 107→ 108→## Features 109→ 110→### Memory Management 111→ 112→| Memory Type | Behavior | 113→|-------------|----------| 114→| **Goals** | Changed only after explicit user confirmation of a scope or requirement change. Versioned. | 115→| **Todos** | Kept in sync with the active goal. Updated when progress changes or next task becomes clearer. | 116→| **Chat Logs** | Append-only. Never rewritten or deleted. | 117→| **Knowledge** | Stores architecture decisions, product ideas, operating constraints. Goals may also hold scope-relevant items. | 118→| **Archive** | Stale context moved here or to knowledge files to keep active files concise. | 119→ 120→### Multi-Client Transcript Import 121→ 122→Import AI chat history from three clients: 123→ 124→| Client | Source | 125→|--------|--------| 126→| **Codex** | `~/.codex/sessions/**/rollout-*.jsonl` | 127→| **Claude Code** | `transcript_path` if exposed; otherwise `~/.claude/projects//*.jsonl` | 128→| **Gemini CLI** | Saved chats first; checkpoints under `~/.gemini/tmp//checkpoints` if enabled | 129→ 130→Key principles: 131→ 132→- Raw transcripts preserved unchanged as source of truth 133→- Cleaned Markdown generated by **deterministic code** (not model tokens) 134→- Current repo only stores transcripts belonging to this project; others go to global archive only 135→- First import asks once whether raw transcripts may be committed to the repo 136→ 137→### Cross-Project Search 138→ 139→When searching all memories or chat history: 140→ 141→1. Fall back to scanning `clean/` directory if the index does not exist 146→ 147→### Session Continuity 148→ 149→When you open a new session and want to continue where you left off, ask MemoryTree to client directories (not waiting for the scheduled heartbeat) 156→2. **Locate** — find the latest session for the current project across Claude Code, Codex, and Gemini CLI 157→3. **Summarize** — generate a continuation summary: what was discussed, what was tried, and what remains unresolved 158→4. **Handoff** — you can say "continue" and pick up exactly where the previous session stopped 159→ 160→This works **cross-client**: a Codex session can be recovered from Claude Code, and vice versa. 161→ 162→For details see [`references/transcript-archive.md`](references/transcript-archive.md) and [`references/heartbeat-scheduling.md`](references/heartbeat-scheduling.md). 163→ 164→### Git Safety 165→ 166→MemoryTree follows strict isolation rules to never interfere with your repo's workflow: 167→ 168→| Rule | Description | 169→|------|-------------| 170→| File isolation | Only stages/commits `Memory/**` (excluding `raw/` until approved) and `AGENTS.md` (when managed by MemoryTree) | 171→| Branch isolation | Uses dedicated branch `memorytree/--` | 172→| Commit format | `memorytree(): ` or repo-compatible equivalent | 173→| PR isolation | Separate PR for MemoryTree-only changes | 174→| Auto-merge | Only when repo still enforces its review and CI checks | 175→| Auto-push | On by default; skips if no remote; fast-forward only | 176→| Protected branches | Never overrides branch protection or CI rules | 177→ 178→The skill stops and asks before proceeding in 8 scenarios: mixed product code, AGENTS.md conflicts, unclear target branch, repo forbids bot pushes, CI conflicts, unknown raw transcript permission, cross-project transcripts, or protected branch refusal. 179→ 180→For the full policy see [`references/git-policy.md`](references/git-policy.md). 181→ 182→### Sensitive Info Scanning 183→ 184→During transcript cleaning, the heartbeat scans for API keys, passwords, tokens, and other secrets: 185→ 186→- Matches are logged as warnings and written to `alerts.json` 187→- **No automatic deletion or redaction** — the user decides how to handle them 188→ 189→### Locale Support 190→ 191→| Feature | Description | 192→|---------|-------------| 193→| Templates | English (`en`) and Simplified Chinese (`zh-cn`) | 194→| Auto-detect | Detects language from repo content first, then system locale | 195→| Reply language | Follows user explicit request > message language > repo locale > template locale > `en` | 196→| Non-destructive | Never rewrites existing files just to translate them | 197→ 198→### Environment Adaptation 199→ 200→MemoryTree does not assume the target repo has a Python environment: 201→ 202→``` 203→Prefer uv run python → fall back to system python → fall back to direct template scaffolding 204→``` 205→ 206→Never auto-creates `.venv` or `pyproject.toml` inside the project. 207→ 208→## Background Heartbeat 209→ 210→The heartbeat automates transcript discovery, import, cleaning, commit, and push without consuming model tokens or requiring human intervention. 211→ 212→**Execution flow**: 213→ 214→``` 215→Acquire lock → Load config.toml → Iterate projects → Discover → Import 216→→ Clean → Commit → (auto_push) Push → Release lock → Exit 217→``` 218→ 219→### Install the daemon 220→ 221→```bash 222→memorytree-daemon install 223→``` 224→ 225→### CLI commands 226→ 227→| Command | Description | 228→|-----------------------------|-------------| 229→| `memorytree-daemon install` | Register the heartbeat with the OS scheduler (cron / launchd / Task Scheduler). | 230→| `memorytree-daemon uninstall` | Remove the scheduled heartbeat task. | 231→| `memorytree-daemon run-once` | Run a single heartbeat cycle immediately. | 232→| `memorytree-daemon watch` | Continuous loop for development and debugging only. | 233→| `memorytree-daemon status` | Show registration state and last execution result. | 234→ 235→### Key settings (`~/.memorytree/config.toml`) 236→ 237→| Setting | Default | Description | 238→|----------------------|----------|-------------| 239→| `auto_push` | `true` | Push to remote after each commit. Skips if no remote is configured. | 240→| `heartbeat_interval` | `"5m"` | Interval between heartbeat executions. | 241→| `log_level` | `"info"` | Log verbosity (`debug`, `info`, `warn`, `error`). | 242→ 243→### Design constraints 244→ 245→- Single execution, stateless, idempotent — safe to re-run at any interval 246→- One computer = one scheduled heartbeat task 247→- Will not silently register — always confirms before `install` 248→ 249→For details see [`references/heartbeat-scheduling.md`](references/heartbeat-scheduling.md) and [`references/global-configuration.md`](references/global-configuration.md). 250→ 251→## Design Principles 252→ 253→| Principle | How | 254→|-----------|-----| 255→| **Zero intrusion** | Never changes the repo's branch model, CI, or review process | 256→| **Minimal dependencies** | Scripts target Python standard library; no third-party packages required at install time | 257→| **Deterministic** | Transcript cleaning done by code, not model tokens | 258→| **Single source of truth** | Each concept defined in one reference file, others cross-reference | 259→| **Spec-driven** | Reference docs define the contract; code implements it faithfully | 260→| **User asset** | Memory belongs to the user; auto_push on by default to prevent local data loss | 261→ 262→## Scripts 263→ 264→All scripts live under `scripts/` and target Python 3.11+ stdlib only (no third-party packages). 265→ 266→### Entry Points 267→ 268→| Script | Purpose | 269→|--------|---------| 270→| [`heartbeat.py`](scripts/heartbeat.py) | Background heartbeat — discover, import, clean, commit, push transcripts | 271→| [`memorytree_daemon.py`](scripts/memorytree_daemon.py) | Daemon lifecycle manager: `install` / `uninstall` / `run-once` / `watch` / `status` | 272→| [`recall-session.py`](scripts/recall-session.py) | On-demand transcript sync and latest session recall for cross-session context recovery | 273→| [`init-memorytree.py`](scripts/init-memorytree.py) | Scaffold `Memory/` and `AGENTS.md` for a new repository | 274→| [`upgrade-memorytree.py`](scripts/upgrade-memorytree.py) | Add missing MemoryTree pieces to a partial repository | 275→| [`import-transcripts.py`](scripts/import-transcripts.py) | Import one transcript into repo mirror and global archive | 276→| [`discover-transcripts.py`](scripts/discover-transcripts.py) | Scan local client stores and mirror matching transcripts | 277→| [`detect-memorytree-locale.py`](scripts/detect-memorytree-locale.py) | Print the effective locale for a target repository | 278→ 279→### Internal Modules 280→ 281→| Module | Purpose | 282→|--------|---------| 283→| [`_transcript_utils.py`](scripts/_transcript_utils.py) | Transcript parsing (Codex/Claude/Gemini), import, SQLite indexing, discovery | 284→| [`_config_utils.py`](scripts/_config_utils.py) | Load/validate `~/.memorytree/config.toml` with `tomllib` | 285→| [`_alert_utils.py`](scripts/_alert_utils.py) | Manage `alerts.json` — dedup, threshold-based alerting, failure counts | 286→| [`_lock_utils.py`](scripts/_lock_utils.py) | PID-based lock file with atomic creation and stale lock detection | 287→| [`_log_utils.py`](scripts/_log_utils.py) | Date-rotated file logging to `~/.memorytree/logs/` | 288→| [`_scaffold_utils.py`](scripts/_scaffold_utils.py) | Template rendering and directory scaffolding helpers | 289→| [`_locale_utils.py`](scripts/_locale_utils.py) | Locale detection and normalization | 290→ 291→## Reference Docs 292→ 293→| File | Purpose | 294→|------|---------| 295→| [`references/project-detection.md`](references/project-detection.md) | Detect install state | 296→| [`references/memory-layout.md`](references/memory-layout.md) | Folder and file naming rules | 297→| [`references/update-rules.md`](references/update-rules.md) | When to version goals, todos, and session logs | 298→| [`references/git-policy.md`](references/git-policy.md) | Git defaults, auto-push, and policy merge | 299→| [`references/heartbeat-scheduling.md`](references/heartbeat-scheduling.md) | Heartbeat architecture, daemon CLI, execution flow | 300→| [`references/global-configuration.md`](references/global-configuration.md) | `~/.memorytree/` layout, `config.toml` schema, `alerts.json` format | 301→| [`references/transcript-archive.md`](references/transcript-archive.md) | Transcript archival, cleaning, search, and upload rules | 302→| [`references/locale-selection.md`](references/locale-selection.md) | Locale choice rules | 303→| [`references/response-language.md`](references/response-language.md) | Reply-language precedence | 304→| [`references/execution-environment.md`](references/execution-environment.md) | How to run scripts without a project-local Python environment | 305→| [`references/upgrade-path.md`](references/upgrade-path.md) | Safe adoption flow for partial repos | 306→ 307→## Update 308→ 309→```bash 310→cd ~/.claude/skills/memorytree-workflow && git pull 311→``` 312→ 313→## Requirements 314→ 315→- [Claude Code](https://claude.ai/claude-code) CLI 316→- Python 3.11+ (for transcript and heartbeat scripts; `tomllib` is stdlib in 3.11+) 317→- Git 318→ 319→## # MemoryTree Workflow **Persistent, Git-tracked project memory for AI coding assistants.** [![License: MIT](https://img.shields.io/badge/License-MIT-blue.svg)](LICENSE) [![Python 3.11+](https://img.shields.io/badge/Python-3.11%2B-3776AB.svg?logo=python&logoColor=white)](https://www.python.org/) [![Claude Code](https://img.shields.io/badge/Claude_Code-Skill-cc785c.svg?logo=anthropic&logoColor=white)](https://claude.ai/claude-code) [![Platform](https://img.shields.io/badge/Platform-macOS%20%7C%20Linux%20%7C%20Windows-lightgrey.svg)](#requirements)

Install  •  Usage  •  Features  •  Heartbeat  •  Docs

--- ## What It Does MemoryTree gives AI coding assistants (Claude Code, Codex, Gemini CLI) a structured memory layer that survives across sessions: | | Feature | Description | |---|---------|-------------| | :dart: | **Goals** | Track your project's north star across sessions | | :white_check_mark: | **Todos** | Version-bound task lists that stay in sync with goals | | :speech_balloon: | **Chat Logs** | Append-only session records for full traceability | | :books: | **Knowledge** | Durable notes, specs, and architecture decisions (also stored in goals when scope-relevant) | | :inbox_tray: | **Transcripts** | Import and archive AI chat history from Codex, Claude Code, and Gemini CLI | All stored as plain Markdown files under `Memory/`, tracked by Git, minimal dependencies. ## Install ```bash git clone https://github.com/beyondchenlin/memorytree-workflow ~/.claude/skills/memorytree-workflow ``` On Windows (Git Bash): ```bash git clone https://github.com/beyondchenlin/memorytree-workflow "$USERPROFILE/.claude/skills/memorytree-workflow" ``` ## Usage In any Claude Code session, run: ``` /memorytree-workflow ``` The skill will immediately: 1. **Detect** — check if the current repo has MemoryTree installed 2. **Alert** — display pending notifications from `~/.memorytree/alerts.json` 3. **Init** — if missing, scaffold `AGENTS.md` and `Memory/` folders 4. **Upgrade** — if partial, add missing pieces without overwriting existing policy 5. **Maintain** — if installed, read the active goal/todo and report current state No manual confirmation needed — the skill acts on the detection result immediately. ## Architecture ### Per-Repository Structure After initialization, your repo will have: ``` AGENTS.md # AI behavior rules for this repo Memory/ 01_goals/ # Versioned project goals 02_todos/ # Task lists bound to goals 03_chat_logs/ # Append-only session logs 04_knowledge/ # (optional) Durable project notes 05_archive/ # (optional) Retired goals/todos 06_transcripts/ # (optional) AI chat history archive clean/ # Cleaned Markdown indexes manifests/ # Import metadata raw/ # Raw transcript mirrors ``` ### Global Directory (`~/.memorytree/`) Cross-project shared state: ``` ~/.memorytree/ config.toml # User-level settings alerts.json # Pending notifications heartbeat.lock # Single-instance lock logs/ # Heartbeat execution logs transcripts/ # Global transcript archive raw/ # By client/project/year/month clean/ # Cleaned Markdown index/ sessions.jsonl # Session metadata search.sqlite # Full-text search index ``` For the full schema see [`references/global-configuration.md`](references/global-configuration.md). ## Features ### Memory Management | Memory Type | Behavior | |-------------|----------| | **Goals** | Changed only after explicit user confirmation of a scope or requirement change. Versioned. | | **Todos** | Kept in sync with the active goal. Updated when progress changes or next task becomes clearer. | | **Chat Logs** | Append-only. Never rewritten or deleted. | | **Knowledge** | Stores architecture decisions, product ideas, operating constraints. Goals may also hold scope-relevant items. | | **Archive** | Stale context moved here or to knowledge files to keep active files concise. | ### Multi-Client Transcript Import Import AI chat history from three clients: | Client | Source | |--------|--------| | **Codex** | `~/.codex/sessions/**/rollout-*.jsonl` | | **Claude Code** | `transcript_path` if exposed; otherwise `~/.claude/projects//*.jsonl` | | **Gemini CLI** | Saved chats first; checkpoints under `~/.gemini/tmp//checkpoints` if enabled | Key principles: - Raw transcripts preserved unchanged as source of truth - Cleaned Markdown generated by **deterministic code** (not model tokens) - Current repo only stores transcripts belonging to this project; others go to global archive only - First import asks once whether raw transcripts may be committed to the repo ### Cross-Project Search When searching all memories or chat history: 1. Query `search.sqlite` for fast full-text lookup 2. Use `sessions.jsonl` for session-level metadata filtering (client, project, date range) 3. Load matching cleaned Markdown for context; confirm exact wording against raw transcripts 4. Combine with the current repo's `Memory/` files 5. Fall back to scanning `clean/` directory if the index does not exist ### Session Continuity When you open a new session and want to continue where you left off, ask MemoryTree to show your most recent conversation: > "Look at my last chat" / "看看我最近的聊天" / "Continue where I left off" The skill will: 1. **Instant sync** — immediately scan all three client directories (not waiting for the scheduled heartbeat) 2. **Locate** — find the latest session for the current project across Claude Code, Codex, and Gemini CLI 3. **Summarize** — generate a continuation summary: what was discussed, what was tried, and what remains unresolved 4. **Handoff** — you can say "continue" and pick up exactly where the previous session stopped This works **cross-client**: a Codex session can be recovered from Claude Code, and vice versa. For details see [`references/transcript-archive.md`](references/transcript-archive.md) and [`references/heartbeat-scheduling.md`](references/heartbeat-scheduling.md). ### Git Safety MemoryTree follows strict isolation rules to never interfere with your repo's workflow: | Rule | Description | |------|-------------| | File isolation | Only stages/commits `Memory/**` (excluding `raw/` until approved) and `AGENTS.md` (when managed by MemoryTree) | | Branch isolation | Uses dedicated branch `memorytree/--` | | Commit format | `memorytree(): ` or repo-compatible equivalent | | PR isolation | Separate PR for MemoryTree-only changes | | Auto-merge | Only when repo still enforces its review and CI checks | | Auto-push | On by default; skips if no remote; fast-forward only | | Protected branches | Never overrides branch protection or CI rules | The skill stops and asks before proceeding in 8 scenarios: mixed product code, AGENTS.md conflicts, unclear target branch, repo forbids bot pushes, CI conflicts, unknown raw transcript permission, cross-project transcripts, or protected branch refusal. For the full policy see [`references/git-policy.md`](references/git-policy.md). ### Sensitive Info Scanning During transcript cleaning, the heartbeat scans for API keys, passwords, tokens, and other secrets: - Matches are logged as warnings and written to `alerts.json` - **No automatic deletion or redaction** — the user decides how to handle them ### Locale Support | Feature | Description | |---------|-------------| | Templates | English (`en`) and Simplified Chinese (`zh-cn`) | | Auto-detect | Detects language from repo content first, then system locale | | Reply language | Follows user explicit request > message language > repo locale > template locale > `en` | | Non-destructive | Never rewrites existing files just to translate them | ### Environment Adaptation MemoryTree does not assume the target repo has a Python environment: ``` Prefer uv run python → fall back to system python → fall back to direct template scaffolding ``` Never auto-creates `.venv` or `pyproject.toml` inside the project. ## Background Heartbeat The heartbeat automates transcript discovery, import, cleaning, commit, and push without consuming model tokens or requiring human intervention. **Execution flow**: ``` Acquire lock → Load config.toml → Iterate projects → Discover → Import → Clean → Commit → (auto_push) Push → Release lock → Exit ``` ### Install the daemon ```bash memorytree-daemon install ``` ### CLI commands | Command | Description | |-----------------------------|-------------| | `memorytree-daemon install` | Register the heartbeat with the OS scheduler (cron / launchd / Task Scheduler). | | `memorytree-daemon uninstall` | Remove the scheduled heartbeat task. | | `memorytree-daemon run-once` | Run a single heartbeat cycle immediately. | | `memorytree-daemon watch` | Continuous loop for development and debugging only. | | `memorytree-daemon status` | Show registration state and last execution result. | ### Key settings (`~/.memorytree/config.toml`) | Setting | Default | Description | |----------------------|----------|-------------| | `auto_push` | `true` | Push to remote after each commit. Skips if no remote is configured. | | `heartbeat_interval` | `"5m"` | Interval between heartbeat executions. | | `log_level` | `"info"` | Log verbosity (`debug`, `info`, `warn`, `error`). | ### Design constraints - Single execution, stateless, idempotent — safe to re-run at any interval - One computer = one scheduled heartbeat task - Will not silently register — always confirms before `install` For details see [`references/heartbeat-scheduling.md`](references/heartbeat-scheduling.md) and [`references/global-configuration.md`](references/global-configuration.md). ## Design Principles | Principle | How | |-----------|-----| | **Zero intrusion** | Never changes the repo's branch model, CI, or review process | | **Minimal dependencies** | Scripts target Python standard library; no third-party packages required at install time | | **Deterministic** | Transcript cleaning done by code, not model tokens | | **Single source of truth** | Each concept defined in one reference file, others cross-reference | | **Spec-driven** | Reference docs define the contract; code implements it faithfully | | **User asset** | Memory belongs to the user; auto_push on by default to prevent local data loss | ## Scripts All scripts live under `scripts/` and target Python 3.11+ stdlib only (no third-party packages). ### Entry Points | Script | Purpose | |--------|---------| | [`heartbeat.py`](scripts/heartbeat.py) | Background heartbeat — discover, import, clean, commit, push transcripts | | [`memorytree_daemon.py`](scripts/memorytree_daemon.py) | Daemon lifecycle manager: `install` / `uninstall` / `run-once` / `watch` / `status` | | [`recall-session.py`](scripts/recall-session.py) | On-demand transcript sync and latest session recall for cross-session context recovery | | [`init-memorytree.py`](scripts/init-memorytree.py) | Scaffold `Memory/` and `AGENTS.md` for a new repository | | [`upgrade-memorytree.py`](scripts/upgrade-memorytree.py) | Add missing MemoryTree pieces to a partial repository | | [`import-transcripts.py`](scripts/import-transcripts.py) | Import one transcript into repo mirror and global archive | | [`discover-transcripts.py`](scripts/discover-transcripts.py) | Scan local client stores and mirror matching transcripts | | [`detect-memorytree-locale.py`](scripts/detect-memorytree-locale.py) | Print the effective locale for a target repository | ### Internal Modules | Module | Purpose | |--------|---------| | [`_transcript_utils.py`](scripts/_transcript_utils.py) | Transcript parsing (Codex/Claude/Gemini), import, SQLite indexing, discovery | | [`_config_utils.py`](scripts/_config_utils.py) | Load/validate `~/.memorytree/config.toml` with `tomllib` | | [`_alert_utils.py`](scripts/_alert_utils.py) | Manage `alerts.json` — dedup, threshold-based alerting, failure counts | | [`_lock_utils.py`](scripts/_lock_utils.py) | PID-based lock file with atomic creation and stale lock detection | | [`_log_utils.py`](scripts/_log_utils.py) | Date-rotated file logging to `~/.memorytree/logs/` | | [`_scaffold_utils.py`](scripts/_scaffold_utils.py) | Template rendering and directory scaffolding helpers | | [`_locale_utils.py`](scripts/_locale_utils.py) | Locale detection and normalization | ## Reference Docs | File | Purpose | |------|---------| | [`references/project-detection.md`](references/project-detection.md) | Detect install state | | [`references/memory-layout.md`](references/memory-layout.md) | Folder and file naming rules | | [`references/update-rules.md`](references/update-rules.md) | When to version goals, todos, and session logs | | [`references/git-policy.md`](references/git-policy.md) | Git defaults, auto-push, and policy merge | | [`references/heartbeat-scheduling.md`](references/heartbeat-scheduling.md) | Heartbeat architecture, daemon CLI, execution flow | | [`references/global-configuration.md`](references/global-configuration.md) | `~/.memorytree/` layout, `config.toml` schema, `alerts.json` format | | [`references/transcript-archive.md`](references/transcript-archive.md) | Transcript archival, cleaning, search, and upload rules | | [`references/locale-selection.md`](references/locale-selection.md) | Locale choice rules | | [`references/response-language.md`](references/response-language.md) | Reply-language precedence | | [`references/execution-environment.md`](references/execution-environment.md) | How to run scripts without a project-local Python environment | | [`references/upgrade-path.md`](references/upgrade-path.md) | Safe adoption flow for partial repos | ## Update ```bash cd ~/.claude/skills/memorytree-workflow && git pull ``` ## Requirements - [Claude Code](https://claude.ai/claude-code) CLI - Python 3.11+ (for transcript and heartbeat scripts; `tomllib` is stdlib in 3.11+) - Git ## License MIT

# MemoryTree Workflow **Persistent, Git-tracked project memory for AI coding assistants.** [![License: MIT](https://img.shields.io/badge/License-MIT-blue.svg)](LICENSE) [![Python 3.11+](https://img.shields.io/badge/Python-3.11%2B-3776AB.svg?logo=python&logoColor=white)](https://www.python.org/) [![Claude Code](https://img.shields.io/badge/Claude_Code-Skill-cc785c.svg?logo=anthropic&logoColor=white)](https://claude.ai/claude-code) [![Platform](https://img.shields.io/badge/Platform-macOS%20%7C%20Linux%20%7C%20Windows-lightgrey.svg)](#requirements)

Install  •  Usage  •  Features  •  Heartbeat  •  Docs

--- ## What It Does MemoryTree gives AI coding assistants (Claude Code, Codex, Gemini CLI) a structured memory layer that survives across sessions: | | Feature | Description | |---|---------|-------------| | :dart: | **Goals** | Track your project's north star across sessions | | :white_check_mark: | **Todos** | Version-bound task lists that stay in sync with goals | | :speech_balloon: | **Chat Logs** | Append-only session records for full traceability | | :books: | **Knowledge** | Durable notes, specs, and architecture decisions (also stored in goals when scope-relevant) | | :inbox_tray: | **Transcripts** | Import and archive AI chat history from Codex, Claude Code, and Gemini CLI | All stored as plain Markdown files under `Memory/`, tracked by Git, minimal dependencies. ## Install ```bash git clone https://github.com/beyondchenlin/memorytree-workflow ~/.claude/skills/memorytree-workflow ``` On Windows (Git Bash): ```bash git clone https://github.com/beyondchenlin/memorytree-workflow "$USERPROFILE/.claude/skills/memorytree-workflow" ``` ## Usage In any Claude Code session, run: ``` /memorytree-workflow ``` The skill will immediately: 1. **Detect** — check if the current repo has MemoryTree installed 2. **Alert** — display pending notifications from `~/.memorytree/alerts.json` 3. **Init** — if missing, scaffold `AGENTS.md` and `Memory/` folders 4. **Upgrade** — if partial, add missing pieces without overwriting existing policy 5. **Maintain** — if installed, read the active goal/todo and report current state No manual confirmation needed — the skill acts on the detection result immediately. ## Architecture ### Per-Repository Structure After initialization, your repo will have: ``` AGENTS.md # AI behavior rules for this repo Memory/ 01_goals/ # Versioned project goals 02_todos/ # Task lists bound to goals 03_chat_logs/ # Append-only session logs 04_knowledge/ # (optional) Durable project notes 05_archive/ # (optional) Retired goals/todos 06_transcripts/ # (optional) AI chat history archive clean/ # Cleaned Markdown indexes manifests/ # Import metadata raw/ # Raw transcript mirrors ``` ### Global Directory (`~/.memorytree/`) Cross-project shared state: ``` ~/.memorytree/ config.toml # User-level settings alerts.json # Pending notifications heartbeat.lock # Single-instance lock logs/ # Heartbeat execution logs transcripts/ # Global transcript archive raw/ # By client/project/year/month clean/ # Cleaned Markdown index/ sessions.jsonl # Session metadata search.sqlite # Full-text search index ``` For the full schema see [`references/global-configuration.md`](references/global-configuration.md). ## Features ### Memory Management | Memory Type | Behavior | |-------------|----------| | **Goals** | Changed only after explicit user confirmation of a scope or requirement change. Versioned. | | **Todos** | Kept in sync with the active goal. Updated when progress changes or next task becomes clearer. | | **Chat Logs** | Append-only. Never rewritten or deleted. | | **Knowledge** | Stores architecture decisions, product ideas, operating constraints. Goals may also hold scope-relevant items. | | **Archive** | Stale context moved here or to knowledge files to keep active files concise. | ### Multi-Client Transcript Import Import AI chat history from three clients: | Client | Source | |--------|--------| | **Codex** | `~/.codex/sessions/**/rollout-*.jsonl` | | **Claude Code** | `transcript_path` if exposed; otherwise `~/.claude/projects//*.jsonl` | | **Gemini CLI** | Saved chats first; checkpoints under `~/.gemini/tmp//checkpoints` if enabled | Key principles: - Raw transcripts preserved unchanged as source of truth - Cleaned Markdown generated by **deterministic code** (not model tokens) - Current repo only stores transcripts belonging to this project; others go to global archive only - First import asks once whether raw transcripts may be committed to the repo ### Cross-Project Search When searching all memories or chat history: 1. Query `search.sqlite` for fast full-text lookup 2. Use `sessions.jsonl` for session-level metadata filtering (client, project, date range) 3. Load matching cleaned Markdown for context; confirm exact wording against raw transcripts 4. Combine with the current repo's `Memory/` files 5. Fall back to scanning `clean/` directory if the index does not exist ### Session Continuity When you open a new session and want to continue where you left off, ask MemoryTree to show your most recent conversation: > "Look at my last chat" / "看看我最近的聊天" / "Continue where I left off" The skill will: 1. **Instant sync** — immediately scan all three client directories (not waiting for the scheduled heartbeat) 2. **Locate** — find the latest session for the current project across Claude Code, Codex, and Gemini CLI 3. **Summarize** — generate a continuation summary: what was discussed, what was tried, and what remains unresolved 4. **Handoff** — you can say "continue" and pick up exactly where the previous session stopped This works **cross-client**: a Codex session can be recovered from Claude Code, and vice versa. For details see [`references/transcript-archive.md`](references/transcript-archive.md) and [`references/heartbeat-scheduling.md`](references/heartbeat-scheduling.md). ### Git Safety MemoryTree follows strict isolation rules to never interfere with your repo's workflow: | Rule | Description | |------|-------------| | File isolation | Only stages/commits `Memory/**` (excluding `raw/` until approved) and `AGENTS.md` (when managed by MemoryTree) | | Branch isolation | Uses dedicated branch `memorytree/--` | | Commit format | `memorytree(): ` or repo-compatible equivalent | | PR isolation | Separate PR for MemoryTree-only changes | | Auto-merge | Only when repo still enforces its review and CI checks | | Auto-push | On by default; skips if no remote; fast-forward only | | Protected branches | Never overrides branch protection or CI rules | The skill stops and asks before proceeding in 8 scenarios: mixed product code, AGENTS.md conflicts, unclear target branch, repo forbids bot pushes, CI conflicts, unknown raw transcript permission, cross-project transcripts, or protected branch refusal. For the full policy see [`references/git-policy.md`](references/git-policy.md). ### Sensitive Info Scanning During transcript cleaning, the heartbeat scans for API keys, passwords, tokens, and other secrets: - Matches are logged as warnings and written to `alerts.json` - **No automatic deletion or redaction** — the user decides how to handle them ### Locale Support | Feature | Description | |---------|-------------| | Templates | English (`en`) and Simplified Chinese (`zh-cn`) | | Auto-detect | Detects language from repo content first, then system locale | | Reply language | Follows user explicit request > message language > repo locale > template locale > `en` | | Non-destructive | Never rewrites existing files just to translate them | ### Environment Adaptation MemoryTree does not assume the target repo has a Python environment: ``` Prefer uv run python → fall back to system python → fall back to direct template scaffolding ``` Never auto-creates `.venv` or `pyproject.toml` inside the project. ## Background Heartbeat The heartbeat automates transcript discovery, import, cleaning, commit, and push without consuming model tokens or requiring human intervention. **Execution flow**: ``` Acquire lock → Load config.toml → Iterate projects → Discover → Import → Clean → Commit → (auto_push) Push → Release lock → Exit ``` ### Install the daemon ```bash memorytree-daemon install ``` ### CLI commands | Command | Description | |-----------------------------|-------------| | `memorytree-daemon install` | Register the heartbeat with the OS scheduler (cron / launchd / Task Scheduler). | | `memorytree-daemon uninstall` | Remove the scheduled heartbeat task. | | `memorytree-daemon run-once` | Run a single heartbeat cycle immediately. | | `memorytree-daemon watch` | Continuous loop for development and debugging only. | | `memorytree-daemon status` | Show registration state and last execution result. | ### Key settings (`~/.memorytree/config.toml`) | Setting | Default | Description | |----------------------|----------|-------------| | `auto_push` | `true` | Push to remote after each commit. Skips if no remote is configured. | | `heartbeat_interval` | `"5m"` | Interval between heartbeat executions. | | `log_level` | `"info"` | Log verbosity (`debug`, `info`, `warn`, `error`). | ### Design constraints - Single execution, stateless, idempotent — safe to re-run at any interval - One computer = one scheduled heartbeat task - Will not silently register — always confirms before `install` For details see [`references/heartbeat-scheduling.md`](references/heartbeat-scheduling.md) and [`references/global-configuration.md`](references/global-configuration.md). ## Design Principles | Principle | How | |-----------|-----| | **Zero intrusion** | Never changes the repo's branch model, CI, or review process | | **Minimal dependencies** | Scripts target Python standard library; no third-party packages required at install time | | **Deterministic** | Transcript cleaning done by code, not model tokens | | **Single source of truth** | Each concept defined in one reference file, others cross-reference | | **Spec-driven** | Reference docs define the contract; code implements it faithfully | | **User asset** | Memory belongs to the user; auto_push on by default to prevent local data loss | ## Scripts All scripts live under `scripts/` and target Python 3.11+ stdlib only (no third-party packages). ### Entry Points | Script | Purpose | |--------|---------| | [`heartbeat.py`](scripts/heartbeat.py) | Background heartbeat — discover, import, clean, commit, push transcripts | | [`memorytree_daemon.py`](scripts/memorytree_daemon.py) | Daemon lifecycle manager: `install` / `uninstall` / `run-once` / `watch` / `status` | | [`recall-session.py`](scripts/recall-session.py) | On-demand transcript sync and latest session recall for cross-session context recovery | | [`init-memorytree.py`](scripts/init-memorytree.py) | Scaffold `Memory/` and `AGENTS.md` for a new repository | | [`upgrade-memorytree.py`](scripts/upgrade-memorytree.py) | Add missing MemoryTree pieces to a partial repository | | [`import-transcripts.py`](scripts/import-transcripts.py) | Import one transcript into repo mirror and global archive | | [`discover-transcripts.py`](scripts/discover-transcripts.py) | Scan local client stores and mirror matching transcripts | | [`detect-memorytree-locale.py`](scripts/detect-memorytree-locale.py) | Print the effective locale for a target repository | ### Internal Modules | Module | Purpose | |--------|---------| | [`_transcript_utils.py`](scripts/_transcript_utils.py) | Transcript parsing (Codex/Claude/Gemini), import, SQLite indexing, discovery | | [`_config_utils.py`](scripts/_config_utils.py) | Load/validate `~/.memorytree/config.toml` with `tomllib` | | [`_alert_utils.py`](scripts/_alert_utils.py) | Manage `alerts.json` — dedup, threshold-based alerting, failure counts | | [`_lock_utils.py`](scripts/_lock_utils.py) | PID-based lock file with atomic creation and stale lock detection | | [`_log_utils.py`](scripts/_log_utils.py) | Date-rotated file logging to `~/.memorytree/logs/` | | [`_scaffold_utils.py`](scripts/_scaffold_utils.py) | Template rendering and directory scaffolding helpers | | [`_locale_utils.py`](scripts/_locale_utils.py) | Locale detection and normalization | ## Reference Docs | File | Purpose | |------|---------| | [`references/project-detection.md`](references/project-detection.md) | Detect install state | | [`references/memory-layout.md`](references/memory-layout.md) | Folder and file naming rules | | [`references/update-rules.md`](references/update-rules.md) | When to version goals, todos, and session logs | | [`references/git-policy.md`](references/git-policy.md) | Git defaults, auto-push, and policy merge | | [`references/heartbeat-scheduling.md`](references/heartbeat-scheduling.md) | Heartbeat architecture, daemon CLI, execution flow | | [`references/global-configuration.md`](references/global-configuration.md) | `~/.memorytree/` layout, `config.toml` schema, `alerts.json` format | | [`references/transcript-archive.md`](references/transcript-archive.md) | Transcript archival, cleaning, search, and upload rules | | [`references/locale-selection.md`](references/locale-selection.md) | Locale choice rules | | [`references/response-language.md`](references/response-language.md) | Reply-language precedence | | [`references/execution-environment.md`](references/execution-environment.md) | How to run scripts without a project-local Python environment | | [`references/upgrade-path.md`](references/upgrade-path.md) | Safe adoption flow for partial repos | ## Update ```bash cd ~/.claude/skills/memorytree-workflow && git pull ``` ## Requirements - [Claude Code](https://claude.ai/claude-code) CLI - Python 3.11+ (for transcript and heartbeat scripts; `tomllib` is stdlib in 3.11+) - Git ## License MIT

## P1: 代码拆分

`_transcript_utils.py` 目前有 **926 行**，职责过重。计划拆分为 4 个模块（每个 ≤400 行）：

| 新模块 | 职责 |
|--------|------|
| `_transcript_parse.py` | 三客户端解析逻辑（Codex / Claude / Gemini） |
| `_transcript_import.py` | 导入、清洗、manifest 生成 |
| `_transcript_index.py` | SQLite 索引操作 |
| `_transcript_discover.py` | 文件发现、项目匹配 |

另外 P1 还包括 transcript 去重修复、CI 流水线（GitHub Actions）、集成测试。

---

## 整体进度总览

| 任务 | 状态 | 说明 |
|------|------|------|
| **P0: 心跳** | 已完成 | heartbeat.py, daemon CLI, 配置/锁/日志/告警模块 |
| **P0.5: 会话延续** | 已完成 | recall-session.py, 跨客户端上下文恢复 |

| **P3: 打包分发** | 未开始 | pyproject.toml, pip install, 跨平台路径 |

还有上次 code review 遗留的几个 **MEDIUM** 级别问题：
- TOML 保存时 Windows 路径反斜杠转义
- alerts.json 读写无文件锁（并发安全）
- daemon `status` 缺少"上次执行结果"
- 日志文件无自动清理/轮转上限
- 文件命名不一致（`memorytree_daemon.py` 下划线 vs 其他脚本用连字符）

    60→def slugify(value: str, fallback: str = "session") -> str:
    61→    ascii_value = value.encode("ascii", "ignore").decode("ascii").lower()
    62→    slug = re.sub(r"[^a-z0-9._-]+", "-", ascii_value).strip("-._")
    63→    return slug or fallback

    66→def sha256_file(path: Path) -> str:
    67→    digest = hashlib.sha256()
    68→    with path.open("rb") as handle:
    69→        for chunk in iter(lambda: handle.read(1024 * 1024), b""):
    70→            digest.update(chunk)
    71→    return digest.hexdigest()

    74→def infer_client(client: str, source: Path) -> str:
    75→    if client != "auto":
    76→        return client
    77→    normalized = source.as_posix().lower()
    78→    if "/.codex/" in normalized or "rollout-" in source.name.lower():
    79→        return "codex"
    80→    if "/.claude/" in normalized or "/projects/" in normalized:
    81→        return "claude"
    82→    if "/.gemini/" in normalized or "checkpoint" in normalized:
    83→        return "gemini"
    84→    raise SystemExit(f"could not infer transcript client from source path: {source}")

    92→    if client == "gemini":

   194→def parse_claude_transcript(path: Path) -> ParsedTranscript:
   195→    records = load_jsonl(path)
   196→    session_id = path.stem
   197→    title = path.stem
   198→    started_at = normalize_timestamp(path.stat().st_mtime)
   199→    cwd = ""
   200→    branch = ""
   201→    messages: list[TranscriptMessage] = []
   202→    tool_events: list[TranscriptToolEvent] = []
   203→
   204→    for record in records:
   205→        record_type = str(record.get("type") or "")
   206→        timestamp = normalize_timestamp(record.get("timestamp"), started_at)
   207→        session_id = str(record.get("sessionId") or session_id)
   208→        started_at = earliest_timestamp(started_at, record.get("timestamp"))
   209→        cwd = str(record.get("cwd") or cwd)
   210→        branch = str(record.get("gitBranch") or branch)
   211→
   212→        if record_type not in {"user", "assistant"}:
   213→            continue
   214→
   215→        message = ensure_dict(record.get("message"))
   216→        role = str(message.get("role") or record_type).lower()
   217→        text_parts: list[str] = []
   218→        content = message.get("content")
   219→        if isinstance(content, str):
   220→            text = content.strip()
   221→            if role in {"user", "assistant"} and text:
   222→                messages.append(TranscriptMessage(role=role, text=text, timestamp=timestamp))
   223→            continue
   224→
   225→        for block in ensure_list(content):
   226→            if isinstance(block, str):
   227→                if block.strip():
   228→                    text_parts.append(block.strip())
   229→                continue
   230→            if not isinstance(block, dict):
   231→                continue
   232→            block_type = str(block.get("type") or "").lower()
   233→            if block_type in TEXT_BLOCK_TYPES:
   234→                text = str(block.get("text") or "").strip()
   235→                if text:
   236→                    text_parts.append(text)
   237→                continue
   238→            if block_type in SKIP_BLOCK_TYPES:
   239→                continue
   240→            if block_type in TOOL_USE_TYPES:
   241→                name = str(block.get("name") or "tool_use")
   242→                tool_events.append(
   243→                    TranscriptToolEvent(
   244→                        summary=f"{name} input={summarize_value(block.get('input'))}",
   245→                        timestamp=timestamp,
   246→                    )
   247→                )
   248→                continue
   249→            if block_type in TOOL_RESULT_TYPES:
   250→                tool_use_id = str(block.get("tool_use_id") or "tool_result")
   251→                tool_events.append(
   252→                    TranscriptToolEvent(
   253→                        summary=f"{tool_use_id} output={summarize_value(block.get('content'))}",
   254→                        timestamp=timestamp,
   255→                    )
   256→                )
   257→
   258→        text = join_paragraphs(text_parts)
   259→        if role in {"user", "assistant"} and text:
   260→            messages.append(TranscriptMessage(role=role, text=text, timestamp=timestamp))
   261→
   262→    return ParsedTranscript(
   263→        client="claude",
   264→        session_id=session_id,
   265→        title=title,
   266→        started_at=started_at,
   267→        cwd=cwd,
   268→        branch=branch,
   269→        messages=messages,
   270→        tool_events=tool_events,
   271→        source_path=path,
   272→    )

   275→def parse_gemini_transcript(path: Path) -> ParsedTranscript:
   276→    session_id = path.stem
   277→    title = path.stem
   278→    started_at = normalize_timestamp(path.stat().st_mtime)
   279→    cwd = ""
   280→    branch = ""
   281→    messages: list[TranscriptMessage] = []
   282→    tool_events: list[TranscriptToolEvent] = []
   283→
   284→    if path.suffix.lower() == ".jsonl":
   285→        payload: Any = load_jsonl(path)
   286→    else:
   287→        payload = json.loads(path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
   288→
   289→    message_signatures: set[tuple[str, str, str | None]] = set()
   290→    first_meta = find_first_mapping_with_keys(payload, {"sessionId", "chatId", "cwd", "branch", "timestamp"})
   291→    if first_meta:
   292→        session_id = str(first_meta.get("sessionId") or first_meta.get("chatId") or first_meta.get("id") or session_id)
   293→        started_at = earliest_timestamp(started_at, first_meta.get("timestamp"))
   294→        cwd = str(first_meta.get("cwd") or first_meta.get("projectRoot") or cwd)
   295→        branch = str(first_meta.get("branch") or branch)
   296→
   297→    def visit(node: Any) -> None:
   298→        nonlocal session_id, started_at, cwd, branch
   299→        if isinstance(node, list):
   300→            for item in node:
   301→                visit(item)
   302→            return
   303→        if not isinstance(node, dict):
   304→            return
   305→
   306→        session_id = str(node.get("sessionId") or node.get("chatId") or node.get("id") or session_id)
   307→        started_at = earliest_timestamp(started_at, node.get("timestamp"))
   308→        cwd = str(node.get("cwd") or node.get("projectRoot") or cwd)
   309→        branch = str(node.get("branch") or branch)
   310→
   311→        role = str(node.get("role") or node.get("author") or node.get("sender") or "").lower()
   312→        timestamp = normalize_timestamp(node.get("timestamp"), started_at)
   313→        if role in {"user", "assistant", "model"}:
   314→            normalized_role = "assistant" if role == "model" else role
   315→            text = extract_gemini_text(node)
   316→            signature = (normalized_role, text, timestamp)
   317→            if text and signature not in message_signatures:
   318→                message_signatures.add(signature)
   319→                messages.append(TranscriptMessage(role=normalized_role, text=text, timestamp=timestamp))
   320→
   321→        tool_name = (
   322→            get_nested(node, "toolUse", "name")
   323→            or node.get("toolName")
   324→            or node.get("functionName")
   325→            or node.get("tool")
   326→        )
   327→        if tool_name:
   328→            tool_events.append(
   329→                TranscriptToolEvent(
   330→                    summary=f"{tool_name} input={summarize_value(node.get('args') or node.get('input'))}",
   331→                    timestamp=timestamp,
   332→                )
   333→            )
   334→
   335→        for value in node.values():
   336→            visit(value)
   337→
   338→    visit(payload)
   339→
   340→    return ParsedTranscript(
   341→        client="gemini",
   342→        session_id=session_id,
   343→        title=title,
   344→        started_at=started_at,
   345→        cwd=cwd,
   346→        branch=branch,
   347→        messages=messages,
   348→        tool_events=tool_events,
   349→        source_path=path,
   350→    )
   351→
   352→
   353→def import_transcript(
   354→    parsed: ParsedTranscript,
   355→    root: Path,
   356→    global_root: Path,
   357→    project_slug: str,
   358→    raw_upload_permission: str,
   359→    mirror_to_repo: bool = True,
   360→) -> dict[str, Any]:
   361→    imported_at = normalize_timestamp(datetime.now(timezone.utc))
   362→    raw_sha256 = sha256_file(parsed.source_path)
   363→    session_label = slugify(parsed.session_id, fallback=slugify(parsed.source_path.stem, fallback="session"))
   364→    artifact_stem = f"{session_label}__{raw_sha256[:8]}"
   365→    source_suffix = parsed.source_path.suffix or ".txt"
   366→
   367→    year_token, month_token = timestamp_partition(parsed.started_at or imported_at)
   368→
   369→    repo_root = root / "Memory" / "06_transcripts"
   370→    repo_raw_path = repo_root / "raw" / parsed.client / year_token / month_token / f"{artifact_stem}{source_suffix}"
   371→    repo_clean_path = repo_root / "clean" / parsed.client / year_token / month_token / f"{artifact_stem}.md"
   372→    repo_manifest_path = repo_root / "manifests" / parsed.client / year_token / month_token / f"{artifact_stem}.json"
   373→
   374→    global_raw_path = global_root / "raw" / parsed.client / project_slug / year_token / month_token / f"{artifact_stem}{source_suffix}"
   375→    global_clean_path = global_root / "clean" / parsed.client / project_slug / year_token / month_token / f"{artifact_stem}.md"
   376→    global_manifest_path = global_root / "index" / "manifests" / parsed.client / project_slug / year_token / month_token / f"{artifact_stem}.json"
   377→    global_event_log_path = global_root / "index" / "sessions.jsonl"
   378→    global_db_path = global_root / "index" / "search.sqlite"
   379→
   380→    for path in (
   381→        global_raw_path,
   382→        global_clean_path,
   383→        global_manifest_path,
   384→        global_event_log_path,
   385→        global_db_path,
   386→    ):
   387→        path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
   388→    if mirror_to_repo:
   389→        for path in (repo_raw_path, repo_clean_path, repo_manifest_path):
   390→            path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
   391→
   392→    if mirror_to_repo:
   393→        copy_file(parsed.source_path, repo_raw_path)
   394→    copy_file(parsed.source_path, global_raw_path)
   395→
   396→    manifest = {
   397→        "client": parsed.client,
   398→        "project": project_slug,
   399→        "session_id": parsed.session_id,
   400→        "title": parsed.title,
   401→        "started_at": parsed.started_at,
   402→        "imported_at": imported_at,
   403→        "cwd": parsed.cwd,
   404→        "branch": parsed.branch,
   405→        "raw_source_path": parsed.source_path.as_posix(),
   406→        "raw_sha256": raw_sha256,
   407→        "raw_upload_permission": raw_upload_permission,
   408→        "repo_raw_path": repo_raw_path.relative_to(root).as_posix() if mirror_to_repo else "",
   409→        "repo_clean_path": repo_clean_path.relative_to(root).as_posix() if mirror_to_repo else "",
   410→        "repo_manifest_path": repo_manifest_path.relative_to(root).as_posix() if mirror_to_repo else "",
   411→        "global_raw_path": global_raw_path.as_posix(),
   412→        "global_clean_path": global_clean_path.as_posix(),
   413→        "global_manifest_path": global_manifest_path.as_posix(),
   414→        "message_count": len(parsed.messages),
   415→        "tool_event_count": len(parsed.tool_events),
   416→        "cleaning_mode": "deterministic-code",
   417→        "repo_mirror_enabled": mirror_to_repo,
   418→    }
   419→    existing_global_manifest = load_json(global_manifest_path)
   420→    manifest = preserve_existing_import_timestamp(existing_global_manifest, manifest)
   421→
   422→    if mirror_to_repo:
   423→        write_clean_markdown(parsed, manifest, repo_clean_path)
   424→    write_clean_markdown(parsed, manifest, global_clean_path)
   425→    if mirror_to_repo:
   426→        write_json(repo_manifest_path, manifest)
   427→    append_to_event_log = existing_global_manifest is None or existing_global_manifest != manifest
   428→    write_json(global_manifest_path, manifest)
   429→    if append_to_event_log:
   430→        append_jsonl(global_event_log_path, manifest)
   431→    upsert_search_index(global_db_path, manifest)
   432→
   433→    result = dict(manifest)
   434→    result["global_index_db"] = global_db_path.as_posix()
   435→    return result

   438→def write_clean_markdown(parsed: ParsedTranscript, manifest: dict[str, Any], path: Path) -> None:
   439→    lines = [
   440→        "---",
   441→        f"client: {manifest['client']}",
   442→        f"project: {manifest['project']}",
   443→        f"session_id: {manifest['session_id']}",
   444→        f"title: {yaml_escape(manifest['title'])}",
   445→        f"started_at: {manifest['started_at']}",
   446→        f"imported_at: {manifest['imported_at']}",
   447→        f"cwd: {yaml_escape(manifest['cwd'])}",
   448→        f"branch: {yaml_escape(manifest['branch'])}",
   449→        f"raw_source_path: {yaml_escape(manifest['raw_source_path'])}",
   450→        f"raw_sha256: {manifest['raw_sha256']}",
   451→        f"raw_upload_permission: {manifest['raw_upload_permission']}",
   452→        f"cleaning_mode: {manifest['cleaning_mode']}",
   453→        "---",
   454→        "",
   455→        f"# {manifest['title'] or manifest['session_id']}",
   456→        "",
   457→        "## Metadata",
   458→        f"- Client: `{manifest['client']}`",
   459→        f"- Project: `{manifest['project']}`",
   460→        f"- Session ID: `{manifest['session_id']}`",
   461→        f"- Started At: `{manifest['started_at']}`",
   462→        f"- Imported At: `{manifest['imported_at']}`",
   463→        f"- Raw SHA256: `{manifest['raw_sha256']}`",
   464→        f"- Raw Source: `{manifest['raw_source_path']}`",
   465→        f"- Repo Raw Path: `{manifest['repo_raw_path']}`",
   466→        f"- Repo Clean Path: `{manifest['repo_clean_path']}`",
   467→        "",
   468→        "## Messages",
   469→    ]
   470→
   471→    if parsed.messages:
   472→        for index, message in enumerate(parsed.messages, start=1):
   473→            lines.extend(
   474→                [
   475→                    f"### {index}. {message.role}",
   476→                    f"- Timestamp: `{message.timestamp or manifest['started_at']}`",
   477→                    "",
   478→                    message.text,
   479→                    "",
   480→                ]
   481→            )
   482→    else:
   483→        lines.extend(
   484→            [
   485→                "No user or assistant messages were extracted deterministically from the source transcript.",
   486→                "",
   487→            ]
   488→        )
   489→
   490→    lines.append("## Tool Events")
   491→    if parsed.tool_events:
   492→        for event in parsed.tool_events:
   493→            lines.append(f"- `{event.timestamp or manifest['started_at']}` {event.summary}")
   494→    else:
   495→        lines.append("- No tool events were extracted.")
   496→    lines.append("")
   497→
   498→    path.write_text("\n".join(lines), encoding="utf-8")
   499→

   501→def upsert_search_index(db_path: Path, manifest: dict[str, Any]) -> None:
   502→    conn = sqlite3.connect(db_path)
   503→    try:
   504→        conn.execute("PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL")
   505→        conn.execute(
   506→            """
   507→            CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS transcripts (
   508→                client TEXT NOT NULL,
   509→                project TEXT NOT NULL,
   510→                session_id TEXT NOT NULL,
   511→                raw_sha256 TEXT NOT NULL,
   512→                title TEXT NOT NULL,
   513→                started_at TEXT NOT NULL,
   514→                imported_at TEXT NOT NULL,
   515→                cwd TEXT NOT NULL,
   516→                branch TEXT NOT NULL,
   517→                raw_source_path TEXT NOT NULL,
   518→                raw_upload_permission TEXT NOT NULL,
   519→                global_raw_path TEXT NOT NULL,
   520→                global_clean_path TEXT NOT NULL,
   521→                repo_raw_path TEXT NOT NULL,
   522→                repo_clean_path TEXT NOT NULL,
   523→                repo_manifest_path TEXT NOT NULL,
   524→                message_count INTEGER NOT NULL,
   525→                tool_event_count INTEGER NOT NULL,
   526→                PRIMARY KEY (client, project, session_id, raw_sha256)
   527→            )
   528→            """
   529→        )
   530→        conn.execute(
   531→            """
   532→            INSERT INTO transcripts (
   533→                client,
   534→                project,
   535→                session_id,
   536→                raw_sha256,
   537→                title,
   538→                started_at,
   539→                imported_at,
   540→                cwd,
   541→                branch,
   542→                raw_source_path,
   543→                raw_upload_permission,
   544→                global_raw_path,
   545→                global_clean_path,
   546→                repo_raw_path,
   547→                repo_clean_path,
   548→                repo_manifest_path,
   549→                message_count,
   550→                tool_event_count
   551→            ) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)
   552→            ON CONFLICT(client, project, session_id, raw_sha256) DO UPDATE SET
   553→                title = excluded.title,
   554→                started_at = excluded.started_at,
   555→                imported_at = excluded.imported_at,
   556→                cwd = excluded.cwd,
   557→                branch = excluded.branch,
   558→                raw_source_path = excluded.raw_source_path,
   559→                raw_upload_permission = excluded.raw_upload_permission,
   560→                global_raw_path = excluded.global_raw_path,
   561→                global_clean_path = excluded.global_clean_path,
   562→                repo_raw_path = excluded.repo_raw_path,
   563→                repo_clean_path = excluded.repo_clean_path,
   564→                repo_manifest_path = excluded.repo_manifest_path,
   565→                message_count = excluded.message_count,
   566→                tool_event_count = excluded.tool_event_count
   567→            """,
   568→            (
   569→                manifest["client"],
   570→                manifest["project"],
   571→                manifest["session_id"],
   572→                manifest["raw_sha256"],
   573→                manifest["title"],
   574→                manifest["started_at"],
   575→                manifest["imported_at"],
   576→                manifest["cwd"],
   577→                manifest["branch"],
   578→                manifest["raw_source_path"],
   579→                manifest["raw_upload_permission"],
   580→                manifest["global_raw_path"],
   581→                manifest["global_clean_path"],
   582→                manifest["repo_raw_path"],
   583→                manifest["repo_clean_path"],
   584→                manifest["repo_manifest_path"],
   585→                manifest["message_count"],
   586→                manifest["tool_event_count"],
   587→            ),
   588→        )
   589→        conn.commit()

   594→def copy_file(source: Path, destination: Path) -> None:
   595→    if source.resolve() == destination.resolve():
   596→        return
   597→    shutil.copy2(source, destination)
