   599→
   600→def transcript_has_content(parsed: ParsedTranscript) -> bool:
   601→    return bool(parsed.messages or parsed.tool_events)
   602→
   603→
   604→def write_json(path: Path, payload: dict[str, Any]) -> None:
   605→    path.write_text(json.dumps(payload, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2) + "\n", encoding="utf-8")
   606→
   607→
   608→def manifest_changed(path: Path, payload: dict[str, Any]) -> bool:
   609→    if not path.exists():
   610→        return True
   611→    try:
   612→        current = json.loads(path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
   613→    except (OSError, json.JSONDecodeError):
   614→        return True
   615→    return current != payload
   616→
   617→
   618→def preserve_existing_import_timestamp(existing: dict[str, Any] | None, payload: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:
   619→    if existing is None:
   620→        return payload
   621→    if manifest_signature(existing) != manifest_signature(payload):
   622→        return payload
   623→    preserved = dict(payload)
   624→    imported_at = existing.get("imported_at")
   625→    if isinstance(imported_at, str) and imported_at:
   626→        preserved["imported_at"] = imported_at
   627→    return preserved
   628→
   629→
   630→def manifest_signature(payload: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:
   631→    return {key: value for key, value in payload.items() if key != "imported_at"}
   632→
   633→
   634→def append_jsonl(path: Path, payload: dict[str, Any]) -> None:
   635→    with path.open("a", encoding="utf-8") as handle:
   636→        handle.write(json.dumps(payload, ensure_ascii=False) + "\n")
   637→
   638→
   639→def load_jsonl(path: Path) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
   640→    records: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
   641→    with path.open("r", encoding="utf-8", errors="ignore") as handle:
   642→        for line in handle:
   643→            line = line.strip()
   644→            if not line:
   645→                continue
   646→            try:
   647→                records.append(json.loads(line))
   648→            except json.JSONDecodeError:
   649→                continue
   650→    return records
   651→
   652→
   653→def load_json(path: Path) -> dict[str, Any] | None:
   654→    if not path.exists():
   655→        return None
   656→    try:
   657→        return json.loads(path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
   658→    except (OSError, json.JSONDecodeError):
   659→        return None
   660→
   661→
   662→def ensure_dict(value: Any) -> dict[str, Any]:
   663→    return value if isinstance(value, dict) else {}
   664→
   665→
   666→def ensure_list(value: Any) -> list[Any]:
   667→    return value if isinstance(value, list) else []
   668→
   669→
   670→def get_nested(payload: dict[str, Any], *keys: str) -> Any:
   671→    current: Any = payload
   672→    for key in keys:
   673→        if not isinstance(current, dict):
   674→            return None
   675→        current = current.get(key)
   676→    return current
   677→
   678→
   679→def extract_text_blocks(blocks: Any) -> str:
   680→    parts: list[str] = []
   681→    for block in ensure_list(blocks):
   682→        if isinstance(block, str):
   683→            text = block.strip()
   684→            if text:
   685→                parts.append(text)
   686→            continue
   687→        if not isinstance(block, dict):
   688→            continue
   689→        block_type = str(block.get("type") or "").lower()
   690→        if block_type in SKIP_BLOCK_TYPES | TOOL_USE_TYPES | TOOL_RESULT_TYPES:
   691→            continue
   692→        if block_type in TEXT_BLOCK_TYPES:
   693→            text = str(block.get("text") or "").strip()
   694→            if text:
   695→                parts.append(text)
   696→            continue
   697→        fallback_text = extract_simple_text(block)
   698→        if fallback_text:
   699→            parts.append(fallback_text)
   700→    return join_paragraphs(parts)
   701→
   702→
   703→def extract_simple_text(value: Any) -> str:
   704→    if isinstance(value, str):
   705→        return value.strip()
   706→    if isinstance(value, list):
   707→        return join_paragraphs(extract_simple_text(item) for item in value)
   708→    if isinstance(value, dict):
   709→        for key in ("text", "content", "value"):
   710→            if key in value and isinstance(value[key], str):
   711→                return value[key].strip()
   712→        return ""
   713→    return ""
   714→
   715→
   716→def extract_gemini_text(node: dict[str, Any]) -> str:
   717→    parts: list[str] = []
   718→    for candidate in (node.get("parts"), node.get("content"), node.get("text"), node.get("message")):
   719→        parts.extend(extract_gemini_parts(candidate))
   720→    return join_paragraphs(parts)
   721→
   722→
   723→def extract_gemini_parts(value: Any) -> list[str]:
   724→    if isinstance(value, str):
   725→        text = value.strip()
   726→        return [text] if text else []
   727→    if isinstance(value, list):
   728→        parts: list[str] = []
   729→        for item in value:
   730→            parts.extend(extract_gemini_parts(item))
   731→        return parts
   732→    if isinstance(value, dict):
   733→        block_type = str(value.get("type") or "").lower()
   734→        if block_type in SKIP_BLOCK_TYPES | TOOL_USE_TYPES | TOOL_RESULT_TYPES:
   735→            return []
   736→        if "text" in value and isinstance(value["text"], str):
   737→            text = value["text"].strip()
   738→            return [text] if text else []
   739→        parts: list[str] = []
   740→        for key in ("parts", "content"):
   741→            if key in value:
   742→                parts.extend(extract_gemini_parts(value[key]))
   743→        return parts
   744→    return []
   745→
   746→
   747→def find_first_mapping_with_keys(value: Any, keys: set[str]) -> dict[str, Any] | None:
   748→    if isinstance(value, dict):
   749→        if keys & set(value.keys()):
   750→            return value
   751→        for candidate in value.values():
   752→            result = find_first_mapping_with_keys(candidate, keys)
   753→            if result is not None:
   754→                return result
   755→        return None
   756→    if isinstance(value, list):
   757→        for candidate in value:
   758→            result = find_first_mapping_with_keys(candidate, keys)
   759→            if result is not None:
   760→                return result
   761→    return None
   762→
   763→
   764→def normalize_timestamp(value: Any, fallback: str | None = None) -> str:
   765→    if isinstance(value, (int, float)):
   766→        return (
   767→            datetime.fromtimestamp(float(value), tz=timezone.utc)
   768→            .replace(microsecond=0)
   769→            .isoformat()
   770→            .replace("+00:00", "Z")
   771→        )
   772→    if isinstance(value, datetime):
   773→        normalized = value.astimezone(timezone.utc) if value.tzinfo else value
   774→        return normalized.replace(microsecond=0).isoformat().replace("+00:00", "Z")
   775→    if isinstance(value, str):
   776→        text = value.strip()
   777→        if not text:
   778→            return fallback or normalize_timestamp(datetime.now(timezone.utc))
   779→        iso_candidate = text[:-1] + "+00:00" if text.endswith("Z") else text
   780→        try:
   781→            parsed = datetime.fromisoformat(iso_candidate)
   782→            if parsed.tzinfo is not None:
   783→                parsed = parsed.astimezone(timezone.utc)
   784→            return parsed.replace(microsecond=0).isoformat().replace("+00:00", "Z")
   785→        except ValueError:
   786→            return text
   787→    return fallback or normalize_timestamp(datetime.now(timezone.utc))
   788→
   789→
   790→def earliest_timestamp(current: str, candidate: Any) -> str:
   791→    normalized = normalize_timestamp(candidate, current)
   792→    current_dt = parse_iso_timestamp(current)
   793→    normalized_dt = parse_iso_timestamp(normalized)
   794→    if current_dt is not None and normalized_dt is not None:
   795→        return normalized if normalized_dt < current_dt else current
   796→    if normalized_dt is not None:
   797→        return normalized
   798→    return current or normalized
   799→
   800→
   801→def parse_iso_timestamp(value: str) -> datetime | None:
   802→    if not value:
   803→        return None
   804→    candidate = value[:-1] + "+00:00" if value.endswith("Z") else value
   805→    try:
   806→        parsed = datetime.fromisoformat(candidate)
   807→    except ValueError:
   808→        return None
   809→    if parsed.tzinfo is None:
   810→        return parsed.replace(tzinfo=timezone.utc)
   811→    return parsed.astimezone(timezone.utc)
   812→
   813→
   814→def timestamp_partition(timestamp: str) -> tuple[str, str]:
   815→    try:
   816→        candidate = timestamp[:-1] + "+00:00" if timestamp.endswith("Z") else timestamp
   817→        parsed = datetime.fromisoformat(candidate)
   818→        return parsed.strftime("%Y"), parsed.strftime("%m")
   819→    except ValueError:
   820→        return "unknown", "unknown"
   821→
   822→
   823→def join_paragraphs(parts: Any) -> str:
   824→    cleaned = [str(part).strip() for part in parts if str(part).strip()]
   825→    return "\n\n".join(cleaned)
   826→
   827→
   828→def summarize_value(value: Any, limit: int = 180) -> str:
   829→    if value is None:
   830→        return "none"
   831→    if isinstance(value, str):
   832→        text = " ".join(value.split())
   833→        return truncate(text, limit)
   834→    try:
   835→        text = json.dumps(value, ensure_ascii=False, sort_keys=True)
   836→    except TypeError:
   837→        text = str(value)
   838→    text = " ".join(text.split())
   839→    return truncate(text, limit)
   840→
   841→
   842→def truncate(text: str, limit: int) -> str:
   843→    return text if len(text) <= limit else text[: limit - 3] + "..."
   844→
   845→
   846→def yaml_escape(value: str) -> str:
   847→    escaped = value.replace("\\", "\\\\").replace('"', '\\"').replace("\n", "\\n")
   848→    return f'"{escaped}"'
   849→
   850→
   851→def discover_source_files(clients: set[str] | None = None) -> list[tuple[str, Path]]:
   852→    requested = clients or CLIENTS
   853→    roots = default_client_roots()
   854→    patterns = {
   855→        "codex": ["sessions/**/*.jsonl"],
   856→        "claude": ["projects/**/*.jsonl"],
   857→        "gemini": [
   858→            "tmp/*/checkpoints/**/*.json",
   859→            "tmp/*/checkpoints/**/*.jsonl",
   860→            "history/**/*.json",
   861→            "history/**/*.jsonl",
   862→            "chats/**/*.json",
   863→            "chats/**/*.jsonl",
   864→        ],
   865→    }
   866→    matches: list[tuple[str, Path]] = []
   867→    seen: set[str] = set()
   868→    for client in sorted(requested):
   869→        root = roots.get(client)
   870→        if root is None or not root.exists():
   871→            continue
   872→        for pattern in patterns[client]:
   873→            for path in root.glob(pattern):
   874→                if not path.is_file():
   875→                    continue
   876→                key = path.resolve().as_posix().lower()
   877→                if key in seen:
   878→                    continue
   879→                seen.add(key)
   880→                matches.append((client, path.resolve()))
   881→    matches.sort(
   882→        key=lambda item: (safe_file_mtime(item[1]), item[1].as_posix().lower()),
   883→        reverse=True,
   884→    )
   885→    return matches
   886→
   887→
   888→def infer_project_slug(parsed: ParsedTranscript) -> str:
   889→    if parsed.cwd:
   890→        try:
   891→            cwd_path = Path(parsed.cwd)
   892→            return slugify(cwd_path.name, fallback="project")
   893→        except OSError:
   894→            pass
   895→    if parsed.client == "claude":
   896→        parent_name = parsed.source_path.parent.name
   897→        parent_name = re.sub(r"^[a-z]--", "", parent_name.lower())
   898→        return slugify(parent_name, fallback="project")
   899→    if parsed.client == "gemini" and parsed.source_path.parent.name:
   900→        return slugify(parsed.source_path.parent.name, fallback="project")
   901→    return "unknown-project"
   902→
   903→
   904→def transcript_matches_repo(parsed: ParsedTranscript, repo_root: Path, repo_slug: str) -> bool:
   905→    repo_root = repo_root.resolve()
   906→    if parsed.cwd:
   907→        try:
   908→            cwd_path = Path(parsed.cwd).resolve()
   909→            if cwd_path == repo_root:
   910→                return True
   911→            if repo_root in cwd_path.parents:
   912→                return True
   913→        except OSError:
   914→            pass
   915→    return project_slugs_match(infer_project_slug(parsed), repo_slug)
   916→
   917→
   918→def project_slugs_match(left: str, right: str) -> bool:
   919→    return bool(left and right and left == right and left != "unknown-project")
   920→
   921→
   922→def safe_file_mtime(path: Path) -> float:
   923→    try:
   924→        return path.stat().st_mtime
   925→    except OSError:
   926→        return 0.0
   927→

1→#!/usr/bin/env python3
     2→"""
     3→Discover local Codex, Claude Code, and Gemini transcript sources and import them
     4→into MemoryTree using deterministic code-based cleaning.
     5→"""
     6→
     7→from __future__ import annotations
     8→
     9→import argparse
    10→import json
    11→from pathlib import Path
    12→
    13→from _transcript_utils import (
    14→    CLIENTS,
    15→    default_global_transcript_root,
    16→    discover_source_files,
    17→    import_transcript,
    18→    infer_project_slug,
    19→    parse_transcript,
    20→    slugify,
    21→    transcript_has_content,
    22→    transcript_matches_repo,
    23→)
    24→
    25→
    26→def main() -> int:
    27→    args = parse_args()
    28→    root = Path(args.root).resolve()
    29→    if not root.exists():
    30→        raise SystemExit(f"root does not exist: {root}")
    31→
    32→    repo_slug = slugify(args.project_name.strip() or root.name, fallback="project")
    33→    global_root = resolve_global_root(args.global_root)
    34→    requested_clients = CLIENTS if args.client == "all" else {args.client}
    35→    discovered = discover_source_files(requested_clients)
    36→
    37→    results: list[dict[str, object]] = []
    38→    skipped: list[dict[str, str]] = []
    39→
    40→    for client, source in discovered:
    41→        if args.limit and len(results) >= args.limit:
    42→            break
    43→        try:
    44→            parsed = parse_transcript(client, source)
    45→        except Exception as exc:
    46→            skipped.append(
    47→                {
    48→                    "client": client,
    49→                    "source": source.as_posix(),
    50→                    "reason": "parse-error",
    51→                    "error": summarize_exception(exc),
    52→                }
    53→            )
    54→            continue
    55→
    56→        candidate_project = infer_project_slug(parsed)
    57→        matches_current_repo = transcript_matches_repo(parsed, root, repo_slug)
    58→        if not transcript_has_content(parsed):
    59→            skipped.append(
    60→                {
    61→                    "client": client,
    62→                    "source": source.as_posix(),
    63→                    "reason": "no-importable-content",
    64→                    "project": candidate_project,
    65→                }
    66→            )
    67→            continue
    68→
    69→        if args.scope == "current-project" and not matches_current_repo:
    70→            skipped.append(
    71→                {
    72→                    "client": client,
    73→                    "source": source.as_posix(),
    74→                    "reason": "unrelated-project",
    75→                    "project": candidate_project,
    76→                }
    77→            )
    78→            continue
    79→
    80→        try:
    81→            result = import_transcript(
    82→                parsed=parsed,
    83→                root=root,
    84→                global_root=global_root,
    85→                project_slug=repo_slug if matches_current_repo else candidate_project,
    86→                raw_upload_permission=args.raw_upload_permission if matches_current_repo else "not-applicable",
    87→                mirror_to_repo=matches_current_repo,
    88→            )
    89→        except Exception as exc:
    90→            skipped.append(
    91→                {
    92→                    "client": client,
    93→                    "source": source.as_posix(),
    94→                    "reason": "import-error",
    95→                    "project": candidate_project,
    96→                    "error": summarize_exception(exc),
    97→                }
    98→            )
    99→            continue
   100→        result["matches_current_repo"] = matches_current_repo
   101→        results.append(result)
   102→
   103→    payload = {
   104→        "repo": root.as_posix(),
   105→        "repo_project": repo_slug,
   106→        "scope": args.scope,
   107→        "client_filter": args.client,
   108→        "discovered_count": len(discovered),
   109→        "imported_count": len(results),
   110→        "repo_mirror_count": sum(1 for item in results if item["matches_current_repo"]),
   111→        "global_only_count": sum(1 for item in results if not item["matches_current_repo"]),
   112→        "skipped_count": len(skipped),
   113→        "imports": results,
   114→        "skipped": skipped,
   115→    }
   116→
   117→    if args.format == "json":
   118→        print(json.dumps(payload, ensure_ascii=False))
   119→    else:
   120→        print(format_result_text(payload))
   121→    return 0
   122→
   123→
   124→def parse_args() -> argparse.Namespace:
   125→    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="Discover and import local AI transcripts into MemoryTree.")
   126→    parser.add_argument("--root", default=".", help="Target repository root.")
   127→    parser.add_argument(
   128→        "--client",
   129→        choices=("all", *sorted(CLIENTS)),
   130→        default="all",
   131→        help="Transcript client family to scan.",
   132→    )
   133→    parser.add_argument(
   134→        "--scope",
   135→        choices=("current-project", "all-projects"),
   136→        default="all-projects",
   137→        help="Import only transcripts related to the current repo, or also backfill other projects into the global archive.",
   138→    )
   139→    parser.add_argument(
   140→        "--project-name",
   141→        default="",
   142→        help="Project label for the current repository. Defaults to the repo folder name.",
   143→    )
   144→    parser.add_argument(
   145→        "--global-root",
   146→        default="",
   147→        help="Override the user-level global transcript root. Defaults to ~/.memorytree/transcripts.",
   148→    )
   149→    parser.add_argument(
   150→        "--raw-upload-permission",
   151→        choices=("not-set", "approved", "denied"),
   152→        default="not-set",
   153→        help="Current repository permission for committing raw transcript files.",
   154→    )
   155→    parser.add_argument("--limit", type=int, default=0, help="Optional limit on discovered source files.")
   156→    parser.add_argument("--format", choices=("text", "json"), default="text", help="Output format.")
   157→    return parser.parse_args()
   158→
   159→
   160→def resolve_global_root(path_value: str) -> Path:
   161→    if path_value:
   162→        return Path(path_value).expanduser().resolve()
   163→    return default_global_transcript_root()
   164→
   165→
   166→def format_result_text(payload: dict[str, object]) -> str:
   167→    lines = [
   168→        f"repo: {payload['repo']}",
   169→        f"repo_project: {payload['repo_project']}",
   170→        f"scope: {payload['scope']}",
   171→        f"client_filter: {payload['client_filter']}",
   172→        f"discovered_count: {payload['discovered_count']}",
   173→        f"imported_count: {payload['imported_count']}",
   174→        f"repo_mirror_count: {payload['repo_mirror_count']}",
   175→        f"global_only_count: {payload['global_only_count']}",
   176→        f"skipped_count: {payload['skipped_count']}",
   177→    ]
   178→
   179→    imports = payload["imports"]
   180→    if isinstance(imports, list) and imports:
   181→        lines.append("imports:")
   182→        for item in imports[:10]:
   183→            if isinstance(item, dict):
   184→                lines.append(
   185→                    f"- {item.get('client')} {item.get('project')} {item.get('session_id')} repo_mirror={str(item.get('matches_current_repo')).lower()}"
   186→                )
   187→        if len(imports) > 10:
   188→            lines.append(f"- ... {len(imports) - 10} more")
   189→    return "\n".join(lines)
   190→
   191→
   192→def summarize_exception(exc: Exception) -> str:
   193→    return str(exc).strip() or exc.__class__.__name__
   194→
   195→
   196→if __name__ == "__main__":
   197→    raise SystemExit(main())
   198→

   375→class ImportTranscriptTests(unittest.TestCase):
   376→    def test_full_import_pipeline(self) -> None:
   377→        with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmp:
   378→            root = Path(tmp) / "repo"
   379→            global_root = Path(tmp) / "global"
   380→            root.mkdir()
   381→            global_root.mkdir()
   382→
   383→            source = Path(tmp) / "source.jsonl"
   384→            records = [
   385→                {"type": "user", "sessionId": "sess-1", "cwd": str(root),
   386→                 "message": {"role": "user", "content": "Test message"}},
   387→                {"type": "assistant", "sessionId": "sess-1",
   388→                 "message": {"role": "assistant", "content": "Response"}},
   389→            ]
   390→            source.write_text("\n".join(json.dumps(r) for r in records), encoding="utf-8")
   391→
   392→            parsed = parse_claude_transcript(source)
   393→            result = import_transcript(
   394→                parsed=parsed,
   395→                root=root,
   396→                global_root=global_root,
   397→                project_slug="test-project",
   398→                raw_upload_permission="not-set",
   399→                mirror_to_repo=True,
   400→            )
   401→
   402→            self.assertEqual(result["client"], "claude")
   403→            self.assertEqual(result["project"], "test-project")
   404→            self.assertEqual(result["message_count"], 2)
   405→            self.assertTrue(Path(result["global_raw_path"]).exists())
   406→            self.assertTrue(Path(result["global_clean_path"]).exists())
   407→            self.assertTrue((root / result["repo_raw_path"]).exists())
   408→            self.assertTrue((root / result["repo_clean_path"]).exists())
   409→
   410→    def test_global_only_import(self) -> None:
   411→        with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmp:
   412→            root = Path(tmp) / "repo"
   413→            global_root = Path(tmp) / "global"
   414→            root.mkdir()
   415→            global_root.mkdir()
   416→
   417→            source = Path(tmp) / "source.jsonl"
   418→            records = [
   419→                {"type": "user", "message": {"role": "user", "content": "Test"}},
   420→            ]
   421→            source.write_text("\n".join(json.dumps(r) for r in records), encoding="utf-8")
   422→
   423→            parsed = parse_claude_transcript(source)
   424→            result = import_transcript(
   425→                parsed=parsed,
   426→                root=root,
   427→                global_root=global_root,
   428→                project_slug="other-project",
   429→                raw_upload_permission="not-applicable",
   430→                mirror_to_repo=False,
   431→            )
   432→
   433→            self.assertFalse(result["repo_mirror_enabled"])
   434→            self.assertTrue(Path(result["global_raw_path"]).exists())
   435→            self.assertEqual(result["repo_raw_path"], "")
   436→
   437→    def test_idempotent_reimport(self) -> None:
   438→        with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmp:
   439→            root = Path(tmp) / "repo"
   440→            global_root = Path(tmp) / "global"
   441→            root.mkdir()
   442→            global_root.mkdir()
   443→
   444→            source = Path(tmp) / "source.jsonl"
   445→            records = [
   446→                {"type": "user", "message": {"role": "user", "content": "Test"}},
   447→            ]
   448→            source.write_text("\n".join(json.dumps(r) for r in records), encoding="utf-8")
   449→
   450→            parsed = parse_claude_transcript(source)
   451→            result1 = import_transcript(
   452→                parsed=parsed, root=root, global_root=global_root,
   453→                project_slug="test", raw_upload_permission="not-set", mirror_to_repo=True,
   454→            )
   455→            result2 = import_transcript(
   456→                parsed=parsed, root=root, global_root=global_root,
   457→                project_slug="test", raw_upload_permission="not-set", mirror_to_repo=True,
   458→            )
   459→
   460→            self.assertEqual(result1["raw_sha256"], result2["raw_sha256"])
   461→            self.assertEqual(result1["imported_at"], result2["imported_at"])
   462→
   463→
   464→if __name__ == "__main__":
   465→    unittest.main()
   466→

1→#!/usr/bin/env python3
     2→"""
     3→MemoryTree heartbeat — single execution, stateless, idempotent.
     4→
     5→Discovers new transcripts across Codex, Claude Code, and Gemini CLI,
     6→imports them into the global archive and per-project mirrors, commits
     7→MemoryTree changes, and optionally pushes. Designed to be invoked by
     8→an OS scheduler (cron / launchd / Task Scheduler) every 5 minutes.
     9→"""
    10→
    11→from __future__ import annotations
    12→
    13→import re
    14→import subprocess
    15→import sys
    16→from pathlib import Path
    17→
    18→from _alert_utils import reset_failure_count, write_alert, write_alert_with_threshold
    19→from _config_utils import Config, load_config
    20→from _lock_utils import acquire_lock, release_lock
    21→from _log_utils import get_logger, setup_logging
    22→from _transcript_utils import (
    23→    ParsedTranscript,
    24→    discover_source_files,
    25→    import_transcript,
    26→    infer_project_slug,
    27→    parse_transcript,
    28→    slugify,
    29→    transcript_has_content,
    30→    transcript_matches_repo,
    31→)
    32→
    33→SENSITIVE_PATTERNS = [
    34→    re.compile(r"(?:api[_-]?key|apikey)\s*[:=]\s*\S+", re.IGNORECASE),
    35→    re.compile(r"(?:password|passwd|pwd)\s*[:=]\s*\S+", re.IGNORECASE),
    36→    re.compile(r"(?:secret|token)\s*[:=]\s*\S+", re.IGNORECASE),
    37→    re.compile(r"(?:sk-|pk_live_|sk_live_|ghp_|gho_|glpat-)\S{10,}"),
    38→    re.compile(r"Bearer\s+\S{20,}", re.IGNORECASE),
    39→]
    40→
    41→
    42→def main() -> int:
    43→    config = load_config()
    44→    logger = setup_logging(config.log_level)
    45→
    46→    if not acquire_lock():
    47→        logger.info("Another heartbeat instance is running. Exiting.")
    48→        write_alert("global", "lock_held", "Heartbeat exited: another instance held the lock.")
    49→        return 0
    50→
    51→    try:
    52→        return _run_heartbeat(config)
    53→    finally:
    54→        release_lock()
    55→
    56→
    57→def _run_heartbeat(config: Config) -> int:
    58→    logger = get_logger()
    59→
    60→    if not config.projects:
    61→        logger.info("No projects registered in config.toml. Nothing to do.")
    62→        return 0
    63→
    64→    logger.info("Heartbeat started. %d project(s) registered.", len(config.projects))
    65→
    66→    for entry in config.projects:
    67→        project_path = entry.resolved_path()
    68→        if not project_path.exists():
    69→            logger.warning("Project path does not exist, skipping: %s", project_path)
    70→            continue
    71→        try:
    72→            _process_project(config, project_path, entry.name or project_path.name)
    73→        except Exception:
    74→            logger.exception("Error processing project: %s", project_path)
    75→            write_alert_with_threshold(
    76→                project=project_path.as_posix(),
    77→                alert_type="push_failed",
    78→                message=f"Heartbeat error for project: {project_path.name}",
    79→            )
    80→
    81→    logger.info("Heartbeat finished.")
    82→    return 0
    83→
    84→
    85→def _process_project(config: Config, project_path: Path, project_name: str) -> None:
    86→    logger = get_logger()
    87→    repo_slug = slugify(project_name, fallback="project")
    88→    global_root = Path.home() / ".memorytree" / "transcripts"
    89→
    90→    # Discover and import new transcripts
    91→    discovered = discover_source_files()
    92→    imported_count = 0
    93→
    94→    for client, source in discovered:
    95→        try:
    96→            parsed = parse_transcript(client, source)
    97→        except Exception:
    98→            logger.debug("Failed to parse %s, skipping.", source)
    99→            continue
   100→
   101→        if not transcript_has_content(parsed):
   102→            continue
   103→
   104→        if not transcript_matches_repo(parsed, project_path, repo_slug):
   105→            continue
   106→
   107→        # Sensitive info scan on cleaned content
   108→        _scan_sensitive(parsed, project_path)
   109→
   110→        try:
   111→            import_transcript(
   112→                parsed=parsed,
   113→                root=project_path,
   114→                global_root=global_root,
   115→                project_slug=repo_slug,
   116→                raw_upload_permission="not-set",
   117→                mirror_to_repo=True,
   118→            )
   119→            imported_count += 1
   120→        except Exception:
   121→            logger.exception("Failed to import transcript: %s", source)
   122→
   123→    if imported_count == 0:
   124→        logger.info("[%s] No new transcripts to import.", project_name)
   125→        return
   126→
   127→    logger.info("[%s] Imported %d transcript(s).", project_name, imported_count)
   128→
   129→    # Git commit + push
   130→    _git_commit_and_push(config, project_path, project_name, imported_count)
   131→
   132→
   133→def _scan_sensitive(parsed: ParsedTranscript, project_path: Path) -> None:
   134→    """Scan transcript messages for sensitive patterns. Warn only, never delete."""
   135→    logger = get_logger()
   136→    for msg in parsed.messages:
   137→        for pattern in SENSITIVE_PATTERNS:
   138→            if pattern.search(msg.text):
   139→                logger.warning(
   140→                    "Sensitive pattern detected in transcript %s (project: %s, role: %s)",
   141→                    parsed.source_path,
   142→                    project_path.name,
   143→                    msg.role,
   144→                )
   145→                write_alert(
   146→                    project=project_path.as_posix(),
   147→                    alert_type="sensitive_match",
   148→                    message=f"Sensitive pattern in transcript: {parsed.source_path.name}",
   149→                )
   150→                return  # One alert per transcript is enough
   151→
   152→
   153→def _git_commit_and_push(config: Config, project_path: Path, project_name: str, count: int) -> None:
   154→    logger = get_logger()
   155→
   156→    # Check for changes in Memory/
   157→    status = _git(project_path, "status", "--porcelain", "Memory/")
   158→    if not status.strip():
   159→        logger.info("[%s] No git changes in Memory/.", project_name)
   160→        return
   161→
   162→    # Stage and commit
   163→    _git(project_path, "add", "Memory/")
   164→    _git(
   165→        project_path,
   166→        "commit",
   167→        "-m",
   168→        f"memorytree(transcripts): import {count} transcript(s)",
   169→    )
   170→    logger.info("[%s] Committed %d transcript import(s).", project_name, count)
   171→
   172→    if not config.auto_push:
   173→        logger.info("[%s] auto_push disabled, skipping push.", project_name)
   174→        return
   175→
   176→    # Remote pre-check
   177→    remotes = _git(project_path, "remote")
   178→    if not remotes.strip():
   179→        logger.warning("[%s] No git remote configured, skipping push.", project_name)
   180→        write_alert(
   181→            project=project_path.as_posix(),
   182→            alert_type="no_remote",
   183→            message="Push skipped: no Git remote configured.",
   184→        )
   185→        return
   186→
   187→    # Push with one retry
   188→    if not _try_push(project_path, project_name):
   189→        logger.warning("[%s] Push failed, retrying once...", project_name)
   190→        if not _try_push(project_path, project_name):
   191→            logger.error("[%s] Push failed after retry.", project_name)
   192→            write_alert_with_threshold(
   193→                project=project_path.as_posix(),
   194→                alert_type="push_failed",
   195→                message="Push failed after retry.",
   196→            )
   197→            return
   198→
   199→    reset_failure_count(project_path.as_posix(), "push_failed")
   200→
   201→
   202→def _try_push(project_path: Path, project_name: str) -> bool:
   203→    try:
   204→        _git(project_path, "push")
   205→        get_logger().info("[%s] Pushed successfully.", project_name)
   206→        return True
   207→    except subprocess.CalledProcessError:
   208→        return False
   209→
   210→
   211→def _git(cwd: Path, *args: str) -> str:
   212→    result = subprocess.run(
   213→        ["git", *args],
   214→        cwd=cwd,
   215→        capture_output=True,
   216→        text=True,
   217→        timeout=120,
   218→    )
   219→    if result.returncode != 0 and args[0] not in ("status", "remote"):
   220→        raise subprocess.CalledProcessError(
   221→            result.returncode, ["git", *args], result.stdout, result.stderr
   222→        )
   223→    return result.stdout
   224→
   225→
   226→if __name__ == "__main__":
   227→    sys.exit(main())
   228→

1→#!/usr/bin/env python3
     2→"""
     3→recall-session — On-demand transcript sync + latest session lookup.
     4→
     5→Scans all three client transcript directories for the current project,
     6→imports any new transcripts, queries the global index to find the most
     7→recent previous session, and outputs its metadata and clean transcript
     8→path so the model can generate a continuation summary.
     9→
    10→Usage:
    11→  uv run python recall-session.py --root /path/to/repo
    12→  uv run python recall-session.py --root . --activation-time 2026-03-15T10:00:00Z
    13→"""
    14→
    15→from __future__ import annotations
    16→
    17→import argparse
    18→import json
    19→import sqlite3
    20→import sys
    21→from datetime import datetime, timezone
    22→from pathlib import Path
    23→
    24→from _transcript_utils import (
    25→    default_global_transcript_root,
    26→    discover_source_files,
    27→    import_transcript,
    28→    infer_project_slug,
    29→    parse_transcript,
    30→    slugify,
    31→    transcript_has_content,
    32→    transcript_matches_repo,
    33→)
    34→
    35→
    36→def main() -> int:
    37→    args = parse_args()
    38→    root = Path(args.root).resolve()
    39→    if not root.exists():
    40→        raise SystemExit(f"root does not exist: {root}")
    41→
    42→    repo_slug = slugify(args.project_name.strip() or root.name, fallback="project")
    43→    global_root = resolve_global_root(args.global_root)
    44→    activation_time = args.activation_time or _now_iso()
    45→
    46→    # Step 1: On-demand sync — discover and import for current project only
    47→    imported = _sync_current_project(root, repo_slug, global_root)
    48→
    49→    # Step 2: Query global index for latest session
    50→    session = _find_latest_session(global_root, root, repo_slug, activation_time)
    51→
    52→    if session is None:
    53→        payload = {
    54→            "found": False,
    55→            "project": repo_slug,
    56→            "repo": root.as_posix(),
    57→            "imported_count": imported,
    58→            "message": "No previous session found for this project.",
    59→        }
    60→    else:
    61→        clean_path = session.get("global_clean_path", "")
    62→        clean_content = ""
    63→        if clean_path and Path(clean_path).exists():
    64→            try:
    65→                clean_content = Path(clean_path).read_text(encoding="utf-8")
    66→            except OSError:
    67→                pass
    68→
    69→        payload = {
    70→            "found": True,
    71→            "project": repo_slug,
    72→            "repo": root.as_posix(),
    73→            "imported_count": imported,
    74→            "client": session.get("client", ""),
    75→            "session_id": session.get("session_id", ""),
    76→            "title": session.get("title", ""),
    77→            "started_at": session.get("started_at", ""),
    78→            "cwd": session.get("cwd", ""),
    79→            "branch": session.get("branch", ""),
    80→            "message_count": session.get("message_count", 0),
    81→            "tool_event_count": session.get("tool_event_count", 0),
    82→            "global_clean_path": clean_path,
    83→            "clean_content": clean_content,
    84→        }
    85→
    86→    if args.format == "json":
    87→        print(json.dumps(payload, ensure_ascii=False))
    88→    else:
    89→        print(format_text(payload))
    90→    return 0
    91→
    92→
    93→def _sync_current_project(root: Path, repo_slug: str, global_root: Path) -> int:
    94→    """Discover and import transcripts for the current project only."""
    95→    discovered = discover_source_files()
    96→    imported = 0
    97→
    98→    for client, source in discovered:
    99→        try:
   100→            parsed = parse_transcript(client, source)
   101→        except Exception:
   102→            continue
   103→
   104→        if not transcript_has_content(parsed):
   105→            continue
   106→
   107→        if not transcript_matches_repo(parsed, root, repo_slug):
   108→            continue
   109→
   110→        try:
   111→            import_transcript(
   112→                parsed=parsed,
   113→                root=root,
   114→                global_root=global_root,
   115→                project_slug=repo_slug,
   116→                raw_upload_permission="not-set",
   117→                mirror_to_repo=True,
   118→            )
   119→            imported += 1
   120→        except Exception:
   121→            continue
   122→
   123→    return imported
   124→
   125→
   126→def _find_latest_session(
   127→    global_root: Path,
   128→    root: Path,
   129→    repo_slug: str,
   130→    activation_time: str,
   131→) -> dict | None:
   132→    """Query the SQLite index for the latest session before activation_time."""
   133→    db_path = global_root / "index" / "search.sqlite"
   134→    if not db_path.exists():
   135→        # Fallback: try sessions.jsonl
   136→        return _find_latest_from_jsonl(global_root, root, repo_slug, activation_time)
   137→
   138→    try:
   139→        conn = sqlite3.connect(db_path)
   140→        try:
   141→            conn.row_factory = sqlite3.Row
   142→            cursor = conn.execute(
   143→                """
   144→                SELECT * FROM transcripts
   145→                WHERE started_at < ?
   146→                ORDER BY started_at DESC
   147→                LIMIT 20
   148→                """,
   149→                (activation_time,),
   150→            )
   151→            rows = cursor.fetchall()
   152→        finally:
   153→            conn.close()
   154→    except (sqlite3.Error, OSError):
   155→        return _find_latest_from_jsonl(global_root, root, repo_slug, activation_time)
   156→
   157→    resolved_root = root.resolve().as_posix()
   158→    for row in rows:
   159→        row_dict = dict(row)
   160→        cwd = row_dict.get("cwd", "")
   161→        project = row_dict.get("project", "")
   162→        # Match by cwd path or project slug
   163→        if _cwd_matches(cwd, resolved_root) or project == repo_slug:
   164→            return row_dict
   165→
   166→    return None
   167→
   168→
   169→def _find_latest_from_jsonl(
   170→    global_root: Path,
   171→    root: Path,
   172→    repo_slug: str,
   173→    activation_time: str,
   174→) -> dict | None:
   175→    """Fallback: scan sessions.jsonl when SQLite index doesn't exist."""
   176→    jsonl_path = global_root / "index" / "sessions.jsonl"
   177→    if not jsonl_path.exists():
   178→        return None
   179→
   180→    resolved_root = root.resolve().as_posix()
   181→    candidates: list[dict] = []
   182→
   183→    try:
   184→        with jsonl_path.open("r", encoding="utf-8") as fh:
   185→            for line in fh:
   186→                line = line.strip()
   187→                if not line:
   188→                    continue
   189→                try:
   190→                    entry = json.loads(line)
   191→                except json.JSONDecodeError:
   192→                    continue
   193→
   194→                started = entry.get("started_at", "")
   195→                if started >= activation_time:
   196→                    continue
   197→
   198→                cwd = entry.get("cwd", "")
   199→                project = entry.get("project", "")
   200→                if _cwd_matches(cwd, resolved_root) or project == repo_slug:
   201→                    candidates.append(entry)
   202→    except OSError:
   203→        return None
   204→
   205→    if not candidates:
   206→        return None
   207→
   208→    # Sort by started_at descending, return latest
   209→    candidates.sort(key=lambda e: e.get("started_at", ""), reverse=True)
   210→    return candidates[0]
   211→
   212→
   213→def _cwd_matches(cwd: str, resolved_root: str) -> bool:
   214→    """Check if a transcript's cwd matches the repo root (exact or subdirectory)."""
   215→    if not cwd:
   216→        return False
   217→    try:
   218→        cwd_resolved = Path(cwd).resolve().as_posix()
   219→        if cwd_resolved == resolved_root:
   220→            return True
   221→        # Check if cwd is a subdirectory of resolved_root
   222→        return cwd_resolved.startswith(resolved_root + "/")
   223→    except OSError:
   224→        return False
   225→
   226→
   227→def _now_iso() -> str:
   228→    return datetime.now(timezone.utc).replace(microsecond=0).isoformat().replace("+00:00", "Z")
   229→
   230→
   231→def parse_args() -> argparse.Namespace:
   232→    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
   233→        description="On-demand transcript sync and latest session recall for MemoryTree.",
   234→    )
   235→    parser.add_argument("--root", default=".", help="Target repository root.")
   236→    parser.add_argument(
   237→        "--project-name",
   238→        default="",
   239→        help="Project label. Defaults to the repo folder name.",
   240→    )
   241→    parser.add_argument(
   242→        "--global-root",
   243→        default="",
   244→        help="Override the global transcript root. Defaults to ~/.memorytree/transcripts.",
   245→    )
   246→    parser.add_argument(
   247→        "--activation-time",
   248→        default="",
   249→        help="ISO timestamp of session activation. Transcripts started at or after this time are excluded.",
   250→    )
   251→    parser.add_argument(
   252→        "--format",
   253→        choices=("text", "json"),
   254→        default="text",
   255→        help="Output format.",
   256→    )
   257→    return parser.parse_args()
   258→
   259→
   260→def resolve_global_root(path_value: str) -> Path:
   261→    if path_value:
   262→        return Path(path_value).expanduser().resolve()
   263→    return default_global_transcript_root()
   264→
   265→
   266→def format_text(payload: dict) -> str:
   267→    if not payload.get("found"):
   268→        return (
   269→            f"project: {payload.get('project', '?')}\n"
   270→            f"imported: {payload.get('imported_count', 0)}\n"
   271→            f"result: {payload.get('message', 'No previous session found.')}"
   272→        )
   273→
   274→    lines = [
   275→        f"project: {payload.get('project', '')}",
   276→        f"client: {payload.get('client', '')}",
   277→        f"session_id: {payload.get('session_id', '')}",
   278→        f"title: {payload.get('title', '')}",
   279→        f"started_at: {payload.get('started_at', '')}",
   280→        f"cwd: {payload.get('cwd', '')}",
   281→        f"branch: {payload.get('branch', '')}",
   282→        f"messages: {payload.get('message_count', 0)}",
   283→        f"tool_events: {payload.get('tool_event_count', 0)}",
   284→        f"imported_this_sync: {payload.get('imported_count', 0)}",
   285→        f"clean_transcript: {payload.get('global_clean_path', '')}",
   286→    ]
   287→
   288→    content = payload.get("clean_content", "")
   289→    if content:
   290→        lines.append("")
   291→        lines.append("--- clean transcript content ---")
   292→        lines.append(content)
   293→
   294→    return "\n".join(lines)
   295→
   296→
   297→if __name__ == "__main__":
   298→    sys.exit(main())
   299→

1→#!/usr/bin/env python3
     2→"""
     3→Import one local AI transcript into MemoryTree using deterministic code-based
     4→cleaning. Current-project sources mirror into both the repo and the user-level
     5→global archive; unrelated sources archive globally only.
     6→"""
     7→
     8→from __future__ import annotations
     9→
    10→import argparse
    11→import json
    12→from pathlib import Path
    13→
    14→from _transcript_utils import (
    15→    CLIENTS,
    16→    default_global_transcript_root,
    17→    infer_client,
    18→    infer_project_slug,
    19→    import_transcript,
    20→    parse_transcript,
    21→    slugify,
    22→    transcript_has_content,
    23→    transcript_matches_repo,
    24→)
    25→
    26→
    27→def main() -> int:
    28→    args = parse_args()
    29→    root = Path(args.root).resolve()
    30→    source = Path(args.source).resolve()
    31→    if not root.exists():
    32→        raise SystemExit(f"root does not exist: {root}")
    33→    if not source.exists():
    34→        raise SystemExit(f"source transcript does not exist: {source}")
    35→
    36→    client = infer_client(args.client, source)
    37→    global_root = resolve_global_root(args.global_root)
    38→    repo_slug = slugify(args.project_name.strip() or root.name, fallback="project")
    39→
    40→    parsed = parse_transcript(client, source)
    41→    if not transcript_has_content(parsed):
    42→        raise SystemExit(f"source transcript does not contain any importable messages or tool events: {source}")
    43→    detected_project = infer_project_slug(parsed)
    44→    matches_current_repo = transcript_matches_repo(parsed, root, repo_slug)
    45→    result = import_transcript(
    46→        parsed=parsed,
    47→        root=root,
    48→        global_root=global_root,
    49→        project_slug=repo_slug if matches_current_repo else detected_project,
    50→        raw_upload_permission=args.raw_upload_permission if matches_current_repo else "not-applicable",
    51→        mirror_to_repo=matches_current_repo,
    52→    )
    53→    result["matches_current_repo"] = matches_current_repo
    54→    result["detected_project"] = detected_project
    55→
    56→    if args.format == "json":
    57→        print(json.dumps(result, ensure_ascii=False))
    58→    else:
    59→        print(format_result_text(result))
    60→    return 0
    61→
    62→
    63→def parse_args() -> argparse.Namespace:
    64→    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="Import one local transcript into MemoryTree archives.")
    65→    parser.add_argument("--root", default=".", help="Target repository root.")
    66→    parser.add_argument("--source", required=True, help="Raw transcript source file path.")
    67→    parser.add_argument(
    68→        "--client",
    69→        choices=("auto", *sorted(CLIENTS)),
    70→        default="auto",
    71→        help="Transcript client family.",
    72→    )
    73→    parser.add_argument(
    74→        "--project-name",
    75→        default="",
    76→        help="Project label used for the global transcript archive. Defaults to the repo folder name.",
    77→    )
    78→    parser.add_argument(
    79→        "--global-root",
    80→        default="",
    81→        help="Override the user-level global transcript root. Defaults to ~/.memorytree/transcripts.",
    82→    )
    83→    parser.add_argument(
    84→        "--raw-upload-permission",
    85→        choices=("not-set", "approved", "denied"),
    86→        default="not-set",
    87→        help="Current repository permission for committing raw transcript files.",
    88→    )
    89→    parser.add_argument("--format", choices=("text", "json"), default="text", help="Output format.")
    90→    return parser.parse_args()
    91→
    92→
    93→def resolve_global_root(path_value: str) -> Path:
    94→    if path_value:
    95→        return Path(path_value).expanduser().resolve()
    96→    return default_global_transcript_root()
    97→
    98→
    99→def format_result_text(result: dict[str, object]) -> str:
   100→    lines = [
   101→        f"client: {result['client']}",
   102→        f"project: {result['project']}",
   103→        f"session_id: {result['session_id']}",
   104→        f"started_at: {result['started_at']}",
   105→        f"detected_project: {result['detected_project']}",
   106→        f"matches_current_repo: {str(result['matches_current_repo']).lower()}",
   107→        f"raw_upload_permission: {result['raw_upload_permission']}",
   108→        f"message_count: {result['message_count']}",
   109→        f"tool_event_count: {result['tool_event_count']}",
   110→        f"repo_raw_path: {result['repo_raw_path']}",
   111→        f"repo_clean_path: {result['repo_clean_path']}",
   112→        f"repo_manifest_path: {result['repo_manifest_path']}",
   113→        f"global_raw_path: {result['global_raw_path']}",
   114→        f"global_clean_path: {result['global_clean_path']}",
   115→        f"global_index_db: {result['global_index_db']}",
   116→    ]
   117→    return "\n".join(lines)
   118→
   119→
   120→if __name__ == "__main__":
   121→    raise SystemExit(main())
   122→

- Functions: `import_transcript()`, `transcript_has_content()`

**`_transcript_discover.py`:**
- Functions: `discover_source_files()`, `infer_project_slug()`, `transcript_matches_repo()`, `default_global_transcript_root()`
- Constants: `CLIENTS`

**`_transcript_index.py`:**
- Functions: `upsert_search_index()` (internal-only, called by import_transcript)

---

### **EXTERNAL IMPORTERS & THEIR NEEDS**

| Script | Imports | Module Source |
|--------|---------|----------------|
| **heartbeat.py** | `ParsedTranscript`, `discover_source_files`, `import_transcript`, `infer_project_slug`, `parse_transcript`, `slugify`, `transcript_has_content`, `transcript_matches_repo` | parse, import, discover (+ common) |
| **recall-session.py** | `default_global_transcript_root`, `discover_source_files`, `import_transcript`, `infer_project_slug`, `parse_transcript`, `slugify`, `transcript_has_content`, `transcript_matches_repo` | parse, import, discover (+ common) |
| **discover-transcripts.py** | `CLIENTS`, `default_global_transcript_root`, `discover_source_files`, `import_transcript`, `infer_project_slug`, `parse_transcript`, `slugify`, `transcript_has_content`, `transcript_matches_repo` | parse, import, discover |
| **import-transcripts.py** | `CLIENTS`, `default_global_transcript_root`, `infer_client`, `infer_project_slug`, `import_transcript`, `parse_transcript`, `slugify`, `transcript_has_content`, `transcript_matches_repo` | parse, import, discover (+ common) |
| **test_transcript_utils.py** | 33 functions including all parsers, helpers, and data validators | All modules |

---

### **REFACTORING CHECKLIST**

1. **Extract shared utilities** → `_transcript_common.py` (optional, consolidate in parse.py for minimal imports)
2. **Create `_transcript_parse.py`** — Move all parsing functions + their dependencies
3. **Create `_transcript_import.py`** — Move import orchestration + manifest generation
4. **Create `_transcript_index.py`** — Minimal module, SQLite upsert only
5. **Create `_transcript_discover.py`** — File discovery + project matching
6. **Update imports in:**
   - `heartbeat.py`
   - `recall-session.py`
   - `discover-transcripts.py`
   - `import-transcripts.py`
   - `tests/test_transcript_utils.py`

---

### **CROSS-DEPENDENCIES REQUIRING CAREFUL ORDERING**

1. **`_transcript_parse.py` has no external dependencies** (only stdlib + constants)
2. **`_transcript_discover.py` depends on:** `ParsedTranscript` from parse.py
3. **`_transcript_import.py` depends on:** `ParsedTranscript` from parse.py + `upsert_search_index` from index.py
4. **`_transcript_index.py` has no external dependencies** (only stdlib)

**Suggested module import order:**
1. `_transcript_parse.py` (foundation)
2. `_transcript_index.py` (pure SQLite)
3. `_transcript_discover.py` (depends on ParsedTranscript)
4. `_transcript_import.py` (depends on both parse and index)

---

This analysis preserves all functionality, maintains clear boundaries, and keeps the external API intact while reducing file complexity from 926 to approximately 200-250 lines per module.

Actually, looking at this more carefully, there are quite a few shared utilities scattered across the codebase. I'm weighing two approaches: either consolidate everything into `_transcript_parse.py` as the foundation layer, or create a dedicated `_transcript_common.py` module for shared utilities alongside the four main modules. from __future__ import annotations

import json
import sys
import tempfile
import unittest
from pathlib import Path


SCRIPTS_DIR = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1] / "scripts"
sys.path.insert(0, str(SCRIPTS_DIR))

from _transcript_utils import (  # noqa: E402
    ParsedTranscript,
    TranscriptMessage,
    TranscriptToolEvent,
    earliest_timestamp,
    import_transcript,
    infer_client,
    infer_project_slug,
    join_paragraphs,
    load_jsonl,
    normalize_timestamp,
    parse_claude_transcript,
    parse_codex_transcript,
    parse_gemini_transcript,
    preserve_existing_import_timestamp,
    slugify,
    summarize_value,
    timestamp_partition,
    transcript_has_content,
    transcript_matches_repo,
    yaml_escape,
)


def make_transcript(
    *, cwd: str = "", source_path: str = "C:/Users/ai/.claude/projects/demo-repo/session.jsonl",
) -> ParsedTranscript:
    return ParsedTranscript(
        client="claude",
        session_id="session-1",
        title="demo",
        started_at="2026-03-14T00:00:00Z",
        cwd=cwd,
        branch="main",
        messages=[],
        tool_events=[],
        source_path=Path(source_path),
    )


class TranscriptMatchesRepoTests(unittest.TestCase):
    def test_matches_exact_repo_cwd(self) -> None:
        parsed = make_transcript(cwd="D:/work/demo-repo")
        self.assertTrue(transcript_matches_repo(parsed, Path("D:/work/demo-repo"), "demo-repo"))

    def test_matches_child_directory_cwd(self) -> None:
        parsed = make_transcript(cwd="D:/work/demo-repo/packages/api")
        self.assertTrue(transcript_matches_repo(parsed, Path("D:/work/demo-repo"), "demo-repo"))

    def test_rejects_parent_directory_cwd(self) -> None:
        parsed = make_transcript(cwd="D:/work")
        self.assertFalse(transcript_matches_repo(parsed, Path("D:/work/demo-repo"), "demo-repo"))

    def test_matches_exact_project_slug_without_cwd(self) -> None:
        parsed = make_transcript(cwd="", source_path="C:/Users/ai/.claude/projects/demo-repo/session.jsonl")
        self.assertTrue(transcript_matches_repo(parsed, Path("D:/work/demo-repo"), "demo-repo"))

    def test_rejects_prefix_suffix_slug_match_without_cwd(self) -> None:
        parsed = make_transcript(cwd="", source_path="C:/Users/ai/.claude/projects/acme-backend/session.jsonl")
        self.assertFalse(transcript_matches_repo(parsed, Path("D:/work/backend"), "backend"))


class SlugifyTests(unittest.TestCase):
    def test_simple_name(self) -> None:
        self.assertEqual(slugify("demo-repo"), "demo-repo")

    def test_spaces_converted(self) -> None:
        self.assertEqual(slugify("My Project Name"), "my-project-name")

    def test_special_chars_stripped(self) -> None:
        self.assertEqual(slugify("project@v2!"), "project-v2")

    def test_empty_returns_fallback(self) -> None:
        self.assertEqual(slugify("", fallback="default"), "default")

    def test_all_non_ascii_returns_fallback(self) -> None:
        self.assertEqual(slugify("\u4e2d\u6587\u9879\u76ee", fallback="project"), "project")

    def test_mixed_ascii_non_ascii(self) -> None:
        result = slugify("my-\u9879\u76ee-v2")
        self.assertEqual(result, "my--v2")

    def test_leading_trailing_dots_stripped(self) -> None:
        self.assertEqual(slugify("..hello.."), "hello")


class NormalizeTimestampTests(unittest.TestCase):
    def test_unix_float(self) -> None:
        result = normalize_timestamp(1710374400.0)
        self.assertIn("2024-03-1", result)
        self.assertTrue(result.endswith("Z"))

    def test_unix_int(self) -> None:
        result = normalize_timestamp(1710374400)
        self.assertTrue(result.endswith("Z"))

    def test_iso_string(self) -> None:
        result = normalize_timestamp("2026-03-14T09:30:00Z")
        self.assertEqual(result, "2026-03-14T09:30:00Z")

    def test_iso_with_offset(self) -> None:
        result = normalize_timestamp("2026-03-14T09:30:00+08:00")
        self.assertEqual(result, "2026-03-14T01:30:00Z")

    def test_empty_string_uses_fallback(self) -> None:
        result = normalize_timestamp("", "2026-01-01T00:00:00Z")
        self.assertEqual(result, "2026-01-01T00:00:00Z")

    def test_none_uses_fallback(self) -> None:
        result = normalize_timestamp(None, "2026-01-01T00:00:00Z")
        self.assertEqual(result, "2026-01-01T00:00:00Z")

    def test_unparseable_string_returned_as_is(self) -> None:
        result = normalize_timestamp("not-a-date")
        self.assertEqual(result, "not-a-date")


class EarliestTimestampTests(unittest.TestCase):
    def test_earlier_candidate_wins(self) -> None:
        result = earliest_timestamp("2026-03-14T10:00:00Z", "2026-03-14T08:00:00Z")
        self.assertEqual(result, "2026-03-14T08:00:00Z")

    def test_later_candidate_loses(self) -> None:
        result = earliest_timestamp("2026-03-14T08:00:00Z", "2026-03-14T10:00:00Z")
        self.assertEqual(result, "2026-03-14T08:00:00Z")

    def test_none_candidate_keeps_current(self) -> None:
        result = earliest_timestamp("2026-03-14T08:00:00Z", None)
        self.assertEqual(result, "2026-03-14T08:00:00Z")


class TimestampPartitionTests(unittest.TestCase):
    def test_valid_timestamp(self) -> None:
        self.assertEqual(timestamp_partition("2026-03-14T09:30:00Z"), ("2026", "03"))

    def test_invalid_timestamp(self) -> None:
        self.assertEqual(timestamp_partition("invalid"), ("unknown", "unknown"))


class JoinParagraphsTests(unittest.TestCase):
    def test_joins_with_double_newline(self) -> None:
        self.assertEqual(join_paragraphs(["Hello", "World"]), "Hello\n\nWorld")

    def test_strips_whitespace(self) -> None:
        self.assertEqual(join_paragraphs(["  Hello  ", "  World  "]), "Hello\n\nWorld")

    def test_skips_empty(self) -> None:
        self.assertEqual(join_paragraphs(["Hello", "", "  ", "World"]), "Hello\n\nWorld")


class SummarizeValueTests(unittest.TestCase):
    def test_none(self) -> None:
        self.assertEqual(summarize_value(None), "none")

    def test_short_string(self) -> None:
        self.assertEqual(summarize_value("hello"), "hello")

    def test_long_string_truncated(self) -> None:
        result = summarize_value("x" * 200, limit=10)
        self.assertEqual(len(result), 10)
        self.assertTrue(result.endswith("..."))

    def test_dict_serialized(self) -> None:
        result = summarize_value({"key": "value"})
        self.assertIn("key", result)


class YamlEscapeTests(unittest.TestCase):
    def test_simple(self) -> None:
        self.assertEqual(yaml_escape("hello"), '"hello"')

    def test_quotes(self) -> None:
        self.assertEqual(yaml_escape('say "hi"'), '"say \\"hi\\""')

    def test_newline(self) -> None:
        self.assertEqual(yaml_escape("line1\nline2"), '"line1\\nline2"')

    def test_backslash(self) -> None:
        self.assertEqual(yaml_escape("C:\\path"), '"C:\\\\path"')


class InferClientTests(unittest.TestCase):
    def test_explicit_client(self) -> None:
        self.assertEqual(infer_client("codex", Path("any/path")), "codex")

    def test_codex_from_path(self) -> None:
        self.assertEqual(infer_client("auto", Path("/home/user/.codex/sessions/file.jsonl")), "codex")

    def test_claude_from_path(self) -> None:
        self.assertEqual(infer_client("auto", Path("/home/user/.claude/projects/test/file.jsonl")), "claude")

    def test_gemini_from_path(self) -> None:
        self.assertEqual(infer_client("auto", Path("/home/user/.gemini/tmp/checkpoint.json")), "gemini")


class InferProjectSlugTests(unittest.TestCase):
    def test_from_cwd(self) -> None:
        parsed = make_transcript(cwd="D:/work/my-project")
        self.assertEqual(infer_project_slug(parsed), "my-project")

    def test_from_claude_source_path(self) -> None:
        parsed = make_transcript(cwd="", source_path="C:/Users/ai/.claude/projects/demo-repo/session.jsonl")
        self.assertEqual(infer_project_slug(parsed), "demo-repo")


class TranscriptHasContentTests(unittest.TestCase):
    def test_no_content(self) -> None:
        parsed = make_transcript()
        self.assertFalse(transcript_has_content(parsed))

    def test_has_messages(self) -> None:
        parsed = make_transcript()
        parsed.messages.append(TranscriptMessage(role="user", text="hello"))
        self.assertTrue(transcript_has_content(parsed))

    def test_has_tool_events(self) -> None:
        parsed = make_transcript()
        parsed.tool_events.append(TranscriptToolEvent(summary="read file"))
        self.assertTrue(transcript_has_content(parsed))


class LoadJsonlTests(unittest.TestCase):
    def test_valid_jsonl(self) -> None:
        with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmp:
            path = Path(tmp) / "test.jsonl"
            path.write_text('{"a": 1}\n{"b": 2}\n', encoding="utf-8")
            result = load_jsonl(path)
            self.assertEqual(len(result), 2)
            self.assertEqual(result[0]["a"], 1)

    def test_skips_malformed_lines(self) -> None:
        with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmp:
            path = Path(tmp) / "test.jsonl"
            path.write_text('{"a": 1}\nNOT JSON\n{"b": 2}\n', encoding="utf-8")
            result = load_jsonl(path)
            self.assertEqual(len(result), 2)

    def test_skips_empty_lines(self) -> None:
        with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmp:
            path = Path(tmp) / "test.jsonl"
            path.write_text('{"a": 1}\n\n\n{"b": 2}\n', encoding="utf-8")
            result = load_jsonl(path)
            self.assertEqual(len(result), 2)


class ParseCodexTranscriptTests(unittest.TestCase):
    def test_basic_codex_transcript(self) -> None:
        with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmp:
            path = Path(tmp) / "session.jsonl"
            records = [
                {"type": "session_meta", "payload": {"id": "sess-1", "thread_name": "Test Session", "cwd": "/work/repo", "git": {"branch": "main"}}},
                {"type": "response_item", "payload": {"type": "message", "role": "user", "content": [{"type": "input_text", "text": "Hello"}]}},
                {"type": "response_item", "payload": {"type": "message", "role": "assistant", "content": [{"type": "output_text", "text": "Hi there"}]}},
            ]
            path.write_text("\n".join(json.dumps(r) for r in records), encoding="utf-8")

            parsed = parse_codex_transcript(path)
            self.assertEqual(parsed.client, "codex")
            self.assertEqual(parsed.session_id, "sess-1")
            self.assertEqual(parsed.title, "Test Session")
            self.assertEqual(parsed.cwd, "/work/repo")
            self.assertEqual(parsed.branch, "main")
            self.assertEqual(len(parsed.messages), 2)
            self.assertEqual(parsed.messages[0].role, "user")
            self.assertEqual(parsed.messages[0].text, "Hello")

    def test_tool_call_events(self) -> None:
        with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmp:
            path = Path(tmp) / "session.jsonl"
            records = [
                {"type": "response_item", "payload": {"type": "function_call", "name": "read_file", "arguments": "/path/to/file"}},
                {"type": "response_item", "payload": {"type": "function_call_output", "name": "read_file", "output": "file contents"}},
            ]
            path.write_text("\n".join(json.dumps(r) for r in records), encoding="utf-8")

            parsed = parse_codex_transcript(path)
            self.assertEqual(len(parsed.tool_events), 2)
            self.assertIn("read_file", parsed.tool_events[0].summary)


class ParseClaudeTranscriptTests(unittest.TestCase):
    def test_basic_claude_transcript(self) -> None:
        with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmp:
            path = Path(tmp) / "session.jsonl"
            records = [
                {"type": "user", "sessionId": "sess-claude-1", "cwd": "/work/project", "gitBranch": "feature",
                 "message": {"role": "user", "content": "What is this?"}},
                {"type": "assistant", "sessionId": "sess-claude-1",
                 "message": {"role": "assistant", "content": [{"type": "text", "text": "This is a project."}]}},
            ]
            path.write_text("\n".join(json.dumps(r) for r in records), encoding="utf-8")

            parsed = parse_claude_transcript(path)
            self.assertEqual(parsed.client, "claude")
            self.assertEqual(parsed.session_id, "sess-claude-1")
            self.assertEqual(parsed.cwd, "/work/project")
            self.assertEqual(parsed.branch, "feature")
            self.assertEqual(len(parsed.messages), 2)

    def test_tool_use_extracted(self) -> None:
        with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmp:
            path = Path(tmp) / "session.jsonl"
            records = [
                {"type": "assistant", "message": {"role": "assistant", "content": [
                    {"type": "tool_use", "name": "Read", "input": {"path": "/a.txt"}},
                ]}},
            ]
            path.write_text("\n".join(json.dumps(r) for r in records), encoding="utf-8")

            parsed = parse_claude_transcript(path)
            self.assertEqual(len(parsed.tool_events), 1)
            self.assertIn("Read", parsed.tool_events[0].summary)


class ParseGeminiTranscriptTests(unittest.TestCase):
    def test_basic_gemini_json(self) -> None:
        with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmp:
            path = Path(tmp) / "chat.json"
            data = [
                {"role": "user", "text": "Hello from Gemini", "timestamp": "2026-03-14T10:00:00Z"},
                {"role": "model", "text": "Hello! How can I help?", "timestamp": "2026-03-14T10:00:01Z"},
            ]
            path.write_text(json.dumps(data), encoding="utf-8")

            parsed = parse_gemini_transcript(path)
            self.assertEqual(parsed.client, "gemini")
            self.assertEqual(len(parsed.messages), 2)
            self.assertEqual(parsed.messages[0].role, "user")
            self.assertEqual(parsed.messages[1].role, "assistant")

    def test_gemini_jsonl(self) -> None:
        with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmp:
            path = Path(tmp) / "chat.jsonl"
            records = [
                {"role": "user", "text": "Question"},
                {"role": "model", "text": "Answer"},
            ]
            path.write_text("\n".join(json.dumps(r) for r in records), encoding="utf-8")

            parsed = parse_gemini_transcript(path)
            self.assertEqual(len(parsed.messages), 2)


class PreserveExistingImportTimestampTests(unittest.TestCase):
    def test_none_existing_returns_payload(self) -> None:
        payload = {"session_id": "s1", "imported_at": "2026-03-14T10:00:00Z"}
        result = preserve_existing_import_timestamp(None, payload)
        self.assertEqual(result, payload)

    def test_preserves_old_imported_at_when_signature_matches(self) -> None:
        existing = {"session_id": "s1", "imported_at": "2026-03-01T00:00:00Z", "raw_sha256": "abc"}
        payload = {"session_id": "s1", "imported_at": "2026-03-14T10:00:00Z", "raw_sha256": "abc"}
        result = preserve_existing_import_timestamp(existing, payload)
        self.assertEqual(result["imported_at"], "2026-03-01T00:00:00Z")

    def test_uses_new_imported_at_when_signature_differs(self) -> None:
        existing = {"session_id": "s1", "imported_at": "2026-03-01T00:00:00Z", "raw_sha256": "abc"}
        payload = {"session_id": "s1", "imported_at": "2026-03-14T10:00:00Z", "raw_sha256": "def"}
        result = preserve_existing_import_timestamp(existing, payload)
        self.assertEqual(result["imported_at"], "2026-03-14T10:00:00Z")


class ImportTranscriptTests(unittest.TestCase):
    def test_full_import_pipeline(self) -> None:
        with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmp:
            root = Path(tmp) / "repo"
            global_root = Path(tmp) / "global"
            root.mkdir()
            global_root.mkdir()

            source = Path(tmp) / "source.jsonl"
            records = [
                {"type": "user", "sessionId": "sess-1", "cwd": str(root),
                 "message": {"role": "user", "content": "Test message"}},
                {"type": "assistant", "sessionId": "sess-1",
                 "message": {"role": "assistant", "content": "Response"}},
            ]
            source.write_text("\n".join(json.dumps(r) for r in records), encoding="utf-8")

            parsed = parse_claude_transcript(source)
            result = import_transcript(
                parsed=parsed,
                root=root,
                global_root=global_root,
                project_slug="test-project",
                raw_upload_permission="not-set",
                mirror_to_repo=True,
            )

            self.assertEqual(result["client"], "claude")
            self.assertEqual(result["project"], "test-project")
            self.assertEqual(result["message_count"], 2)
            self.assertTrue(Path(result["global_raw_path"]).exists())
            self.assertTrue(Path(result["global_clean_path"]).exists())
            self.assertTrue((root / result["repo_raw_path"]).exists())
            self.assertTrue((root / result["repo_clean_path"]).exists())

    def test_global_only_import(self) -> None:
        with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmp:
            root = Path(tmp) / "repo"
            global_root = Path(tmp) / "global"
            root.mkdir()
            global_root.mkdir()

            source = Path(tmp) / "source.jsonl"
            records = [
                {"type": "user", "message": {"role": "user", "content": "Test"}},
            ]
            source.write_text("\n".join(json.dumps(r) for r in records), encoding="utf-8")

            parsed = parse_claude_transcript(source)
            result = import_transcript(
                parsed=parsed,
                root=root,
                global_root=global_root,
                project_slug="other-project",
                raw_upload_permission="not-applicable",
                mirror_to_repo=False,
            )

            self.assertFalse(result["repo_mirror_enabled"])
            self.assertTrue(Path(result["global_raw_path"]).exists())
            self.assertEqual(result["repo_raw_path"], "")

    def test_idempotent_reimport(self) -> None:
        with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmp:
            root = Path(tmp) / "repo"
            global_root = Path(tmp) / "global"
            root.mkdir()
            global_root.mkdir()

            source = Path(tmp) / "source.jsonl"
            records = [
                {"type": "user", "message": {"role": "user", "content": "Test"}},
            ]
            source.write_text("\n".join(json.dumps(r) for r in records), encoding="utf-8")

            parsed = parse_claude_transcript(source)
            result1 = import_transcript(
                parsed=parsed, root=root, global_root=global_root,
                project_slug="test", raw_upload_permission="not-set", mirror_to_repo=True,
            )
            result2 = import_transcript(
                parsed=parsed, root=root, global_root=global_root,
                project_slug="test", raw_upload_permission="not-set", mirror_to_repo=True,
            )

            self.assertEqual(result1["raw_sha256"], result2["raw_sha256"])
            self.assertEqual(result1["imported_at"], result2["imported_at"])


if __name__ == "__main__":
    unittest.main()

    29→class TranscriptToolEvent:
    30→    summary: str
    31→    timestamp: str | None = None
    32→
    33→
    34→@dataclass
    35→class ParsedTranscript:
    36→    client: str
    37→    session_id: str
    38→    title: str
    39→    started_at: str
    40→    cwd: str
    41→    branch: str
    42→    messages: list[TranscriptMessage]
    43→    tool_events: list[TranscriptToolEvent]
    44→    source_path: Path
    45→
    46→
    47→def default_global_transcript_root() -> Path:
    48→    return Path.home() / ".memorytree" / "transcripts"
    49→
    50→
    51→def default_client_roots() -> dict[str, Path]:
    52→    home = Path.home()
    53→    return {
    54→        "codex": home / ".codex",
    55→        "claude": home / ".claude",
    56→        "gemini": home / ".gemini",
    57→    }
    58→
    59→
    60→def slugify(value: str, fallback: str = "session") -> str:
    61→    ascii_value = value.encode("ascii", "ignore").decode("ascii").lower()
    62→    slug = re.sub(r"[^a-z0-9._-]+", "-", ascii_value).strip("-._")
    63→    return slug or fallback
    64→
    65→
    66→def sha256_file(path: Path) -> str:
    67→    digest = hashlib.sha256()
    68→    with path.open("rb") as handle:
    69→        for chunk in iter(lambda: handle.read(1024 * 1024), b""):
    70→            digest.update(chunk)
